title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
MUST APPLY AT LINK HERE: 



About Us

ValveMan, part of the FSW Group, is a family-owned, solution-focused distributor of valves and instrumentation with over 60 years of experience in the industrial, sanitary, and high-purity markets. We pride ourselves on expert service, high responsiveness, and a collaborative, supportive team culture.



Position Summary

The Executive Assistant provides day-to-day administrative and operational support to the CEO. This role is responsible for managing communication, scheduling, meeting coordination, and executive support tasks to help the CEO stay focused on priorities. The EA also partners with the Team Manager to offboard tasks to other virtual tea
mbers and ensures urgent matters are escalated quickly.



Key Responsibilities



Executive Support & Communication



Hold a brief daily sync with the CEO to confirm priorities and key updates.

Monitor inboxes and messages; read, draft, file/label
s, and respond to
s on behalf of the CEO as needed.

Unsubscribe from unwanted e-mails and or newsletters.

Flag and escalate urgent matters immediately and proactively keep the CEO informed.

Handle ad hoc tasks as requested



Calendar & Meeting Coordination



Coordinate, schedule, and book internal and external meetings.

Send invites, confirm attendees, manage reschedules/cancellations, and ensure meeting details are correct (time zones, links, agendas, and materials).

Join meetings as requested and note action items for each tea
mber through ASANA, request/require due dates for any task

Followup with tea
mbers and vendors on tasks assuring tasks are completed in a timely manner and following up, if required

Set appointments as requested (business and related personal/administrative appointments if applicable).

Set monthly and/or quarterly recurring meetings per schedule



Task Coordination & Delegation



Coordinate with the Team Manager to identify tasks that can be offboarded to other virtual tea
mbers.

Provide clear task notes, context, and follow-ups to ensure delegated work is completed accurately and on time.



Documents, Decks, and Reporting



Create and update decks and presentations (internal and external).

Build spreadsheets, pull basic reports, and complete ad hoc data/administrative tasks as needed.

Build SOPs and offboard as needed.

Summarize information clearly for quick executive review.



Instruction Intake & Follow-Through



Review Loom videos, voice memos, and written notes to capture instructions and convert them into clear tasks.

Confirm next steps, track action items, and ensure completion without repeated follow-up from the CEO.



Required Skills & Qualifications



Strong written communication skills (professional, clear, and concise).

Proficient and confident in speaking English.

Excellent organization and attention to detail; able to manage multiple priorities.

High responsiveness and strong judgment—knows what is urgent and escalates appropriately.

Comfortable supporting a fast-moving executive and handling confidential information.

Proficient with AI tools and common business tools 
, calendar scheduling, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365, spreadsheets, presentation tools).

Advanced skills in building and managing spreadsheets



Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have)



Experience supporting a CEO or senior executive.

Experience working with virtual teams and coordinating task delegation.

Familiarity with task/project tracking tools (e.g., Asana, ClickUp, Trello, 
).



Working Style Expectations



Proactive: anticipates needs and communicates early.

Reliable: follows through and closes loops.

Accountability: willing to hold tea
mbers accountable in a professional and supportive way

Escalation-first: urgent matters are always brought to the CEO’s attention immediately.

title: Project Manager/Dispatcher
description:
About ElasticIT



At ElasticIT, we're building a world where healthcare workers never wait for technology. We're not just another IT support company; we're the team that rescues healthcare providers when technology threatens patient care, turning chaos into clarity and empowering ordinary teams to achieve extraordinary results through the right technology.



We specialise in healthcare IT, providing managed services, custom software development, and HIPAA-compliant solutions that ensure medical professionals can focus on what matters most: their patients, not their systems. Our North Star is audacious yet simple: by 2050, we will eliminate repetitive IT tasks for 1 million healthcare workers, proving that the right technology makes ordinary people extraordinary.



Our Values: The DNA of How We Operate



Our values aren't just words on a wall; they're the blueprint for every interaction, every solution, and every relationship we build:

•SIMPLIFY THE OVERWHELMING: Cut through confusion with clarity and convert half-hour tasks into one-click wins

•RESCUE WHEN OTHERS PANIC: Respond when others would panic and deliver the impossible under pressure

•BUILD LASTING CAPABILITY: Create tools that spread like wildfire and teams that work without micromanaging

•PERSIST THROUGH CHAOS: Adapt when goalposts keep moving and turn disasters into relationships

•ENABLE HUMAN PURPOSE: Remove barriers so the focus can be on patients, not systems



The Problem We're Solving



Whether it's a client transformation project, an internal ElasticIT initiative, or managing the daily flow of incoming support requests, the same coordination challenges have led to real-world problems:

•Helpdesk teams not informed of go-live dates until the morning of deployment

•Missed deadlines due to lack of structured project planning

•Leadership stretched between strategic client work and operational coordination

•No clear ownership for project tracking and execution accountability

•Internal improvement projects losing 
ntum without dedicated coordination

•Tickets flooding in with no clear priority, leaving critical issues buried amongst routine requests

•Healthcare practices waiting whilst technicians figure out who should handle what

•Urgent issues escalating because initial triage missed the severity



The Role

You'll be the person who turns vague briefs into bulletproof plans and ensures nothing falls through the cracks. When leadership identifies an opportunity, whether that's a client transformation or an internal initiative, you're the one who evaluates what's actually needed, spots the gaps others miss, and uses your judgement to assess risks before they become problems. You'll convert ideas into proper project plans with scheduled tasks, clear ownership, and timely communication to all stakeholders.



This role also carries a secondary responsibility: providing backup and cover for our dispatcher function, managing the flow of incoming tickets when needed. This isn't just about covering holidays, it's about understanding the full lifecycle of our client journey, from the 
nt a ticket arrives through to project completion. By bridging both worlds, you'll develop an invaluable perspective on how our service delivery truly works, seeing the immediate client needs alongside the strategic improvements that prevent those same issues from recurring.



This isn't about being the person who comes up with the big ideas or the technical expert who fixes the problems, it's about being the thoughtful evaluator who instinctively knows what's going to work and the dependable force who ensures nothing falls through the cracks. You're the person who prevents the "four hours late" scenarios because you spotted the gap in the plan early, who follows up when others forget, and who takes genuine satisfaction in crossing tasks off the list. When you say something will be done right, it gets done right.



For client projects, you'll support our vCIO function and technical teams to ensure seamless delivery. For internal projects, you'll work alongside leadership and department heads to drive ElasticIT's own improvements forward. For dispatcher cover, you'll be the calm voice that reassures a panicking practice manager that help is on the way, the eyes that spot a P1 hiding in a sea of password resets, and the hands that keep our ticket queue flowing smoothly. In all cases, you're the person who instinctively knows what's going to work and what isn't, evaluating plans, providing steady coordination, and removing blockers so everyone else can do their best work.



What You'll Do

Primary Function: Project Management

•Translate strategy to execution: Receive strategic briefs from vCIO conversations (for client projects) or leadership discussions (for internal initiatives) and break them down into actionable project phases and milestones

•Create comprehensive project plans: Document objectives, scope, stakeholders, milestones, tasks, dependencies, risks, and communication plans for every project

•Coordinate across teams: Brief technical teams before go-lives, consult with TAM on technical considerations, ensure clients and internal stakeholders receive proactive updates

•Drive accountability: Follow up on tasks, remove blockers, escalate issues early, and ensure deadlines are met or communicated in advance

•Maintain documentation: Keep project records, capture lessons learned, and drive process improvements

•Drive internal initiatives: Coordinate ElasticIT's own improvement projects, from process automation to strategic business development activities, ensuring internal work gets the same rigour as client projects

•Enable leadership focus: Free vCIO and leadership to focus on strategic work without operational distractions



Secondary Function: Dispatcher/Triage Backup

•Triage incoming tickets: Rapidly assess, categorise, and prioritise all incoming requests during cover periods, ensuring nothing critical gets lost in the noise

•Provide first response: Acknowledge every ticket promptly, gather essential information, and set client expectations clearly

•Route intelligently: Match tickets to the right technician based on skill, availability, and urgency – not just whoever's next

•Escalate appropriately: Recognise P1 and P2 situations immediately and trigger the right response protocols

•Monitor queue health: Keep an eye on ticket age, SLA breaches, and workload distribution, flagging issues before they become problems

•Support client communication: Keep clients informed of progress, especially during high-priority i



About You

Essential:

•Strong project management fundamentals, planning, scheduling, tracking

•Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal

•Highly organised with exceptional attention to detail

•Ability to ask the right questions and drive clarity

•Ability to stay calm under pressure and maintain clarity when things get busy

•Basic technical understanding, enough to distinguish a network outage from a forgotten password

•Empathy and patience, healthcare workers calling us are often stressed and need reassurance



Nice to Have:

•Experience with PSA tools (we use Halo but experience with any PSA is helpful)

•Healthcare IT background

•Technical certifications

•Understanding of MSP landscape and terminology (can be taught)

•Background in IT support or helpdesk environments



Not Essential:

Deep technical skills. This role doesn't fix tickets; it coordinates people who do. A non-technical person with great organisational skills and a calm demeanour is often better than a technician trying to be organised.



Who you are:

•Someone with a natural instinct for what's going to work, you can look at a plan and immediately sense what's missing or what's about to go wrong

•Able to take a vague brief and quickly evaluate what actually needs to happen, you turn ambiguity into clarity without needing everything spelled out

•Finds genuine satisfaction in completing what you start, an unfinished task list keeps you up at night, and you get real energy from crossing things off as done

•Takes pride in being the person others trust for good judgement, when you say something will work, people believe you because you're usually right

•Gets energy from helping others succeed, whether that's spotting a problem before it derails the team, providing support when someone's stuck, or being the reassuring voice when a client is stressed

•Thrives with clear direction, you don't need to invent the strategy or ask "why are we doing this?", you need to know what success looks like so you can evaluate the best path to get there

•Committed to follow-through, once you've assessed what needs doing and said yes, you're determined to deliver, no matter how tedious the final 10?comes

•Someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, you don't freeze when the pace picks up, you focus

•Naturally empathetic, when a stressed practice manager calls, you genuinely want to help them feel better



What We Offer

•Work that matters: Every project you see through to completion and every ticket you triage correctly directly impacts healthcare delivery and patient care, your follow-through makes a real difference

•Clear direction and support: Leadership who sets clear objectives and then trusts you to deliver, with documented procedures and systematic approaches that reward thorough, careful work

Growth potential: Develop into senior project management, operations leadership, or client success roles

•Recognition for reliability: Work alongside people who genuinely value dependability and follow-through, where being the person who gets things done is celebrated, not taken for granted

•Purpose you can commit to: Contribute directly to our North Star of liberating 1 million healthcare workers from repetitive IT tasks, a mission worth seeing through



THE PRACTICAL STUFF

POSITION: Full-time (40 hours/week), remote

HOURS: Mostly U.S. Eastern Time (8 AM - 5 PM EST) with some flexibility

- Full professional attention must be dedicated to EIT during scheduled working hours — no freelance, consulting, or secondary employment during shift.



READY TO APPLY?

Complete our application form: 
/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=mSS-jteCcUa2SUvZ2Pn71DLj3g8jt9NFjsUkZjVNOzBUOTRQVzRGWjFURTZNSjM2TjFQVlFHWDhYUC4u



WHAT WE WANT TO SEE:

A short video introducing yourself - (1-2 minutes)

Filled out form - 
/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=mSS-jteCcUa2SUvZ2Pn71DLj3g8jt9NFjsUkZjVNOzBUOTRQVzRGWjFURTZNSjM2TjFQVlFHWDhYUC4u

Resume/CV link

Cover letter containing:

Your experience with examples of managing competing priorities

Why you think you'd be good at this type of controlled chaos

title: Manual Quality Assurance (QA) Engineer
description:
Part-Time Manual Quality Assurance (QA) Engineer

Employment Type: Part-Time (10–20 hours per week)

Engagement Type: Independent Contractor

Location: Remote

Department: Engineering

Reports To: Product Owner / Engineering Lead

________________________________________

About Total CSR

Total CSR is a technology-driven company focused on building scalable, high-impact digital platforms for enterprise and professional services organizations. Our work sits at the intersection of product, engineering, and automation. 



As our platform and development velocity grow, we need to strengthen our manual QA capacity to ensure reliable, high-quality releases. While automation continues to expand, manual testing remains critical for validating complex user workflows, payment functionality, subscription logic, and overall user experience, especially during rapid product changes and new feature delivery. This role will help ensure thorough functional testing, regression coverage, and clear documentation of system behavior, supporting stable releases and maintaining customer trust.

________________________________________

Position Summary

The Part-Time Manual QA Engineer will be responsible for validating new features, enhancements, and defect resolutions within a web-based SaaS platform. This role ensures functional accuracy, usability, and release stability through structured manual testing processes.



The QA Engineer will collaborate cross-functionally with Product and Engineering to support sprint deliverables, regression cycles, and production readiness. This position is execution-focused and requires strong attention to detail, independent work capability, and clear documentation practices.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

1. Functional Testing

Execute manual test cases for new feature development.

Validate system behavior against documented requirements and acceptance criteria.

Perform negative testing and edge-case validation.



________________________________________

2. Regression Testing

Conduct structured regression testing prior to releases.

Maintain regression coverage documentation.

Ensure previously released functionality remains stable.

________________________________________

3. Defect Management

Identify, reproduce, and document defects with clear reproduction steps.

Provide expected vs. actual behavior analysis.

Collaborate with Engineering to validate fixes.

________________________________________

4. Documentation & Reporting

Maintain clear test execution records.

Update test cases as features evolve.

Provide QA sign-off summaries prior to release.

________________________________________

Required Qualifications

2+ years of experience in Manual Quality Assurance.

Experience testing web-based SaaS applications.

Strong understanding of functional, regression, and exploratory testing.

Ability to analyze workflows and identify risk areas.

Clear written communication and structured documentation skills.

Ability to work independently in a remote environment.

________________________________________

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in automation testing.

Exposure to API testing concepts (manual validation level).

________________________________________

Working Conditions & Expectations

Flexible part-time schedule (10–20 hours per week).

Increased involvement during release cycles.

Close collaboration with the Product Owner and Engineering team.

Independent execution with minimal supervision.

________________________________________

Performance Expectations

Success in this role will be measured by:

Reduction of production defects.

Clear and actionable bug reports.

Reliable regression validation before releases.

Transparent test coverage documentation.

Timely QA sign-off for sprint deliverables.



________________________________________

Compensation

Hourly compensation based on experience and qualifications.

title: Medical Virtual Assistant
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/2475d4d7-7ed7-449e-a350-75eb5484b5db





What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We are looking for a top-tier Patient Care Coordinator to become an integral part of our expanding team. This role is vital in delivering the best care possible to our patients, handling patient communications, and providing essential support to our clinical staff in their mission to offer exceptional care. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exemplary professionalism, excellent English language communication skills, and outstanding organizational abilities with meticulous attention to detail. This position offers an exciting opportunity to contribute significantly to our patient-centric approach in healthcare delivery.



Responsibilities

• Customize initial discussions based on patient needs, ensuring they understand how the program can address their specific health conditions, providing personalized benefits rather than general services.

• Manage inquiries through multiple communication channels (phone,
, etc.), including voi
 inbox management and message routing, providing prompt and accurate responses.

• Process and manage referrals, working towards efficient conversion within a 7-10 day timeframe while managing multiple referral loop processes for a large patient panel.

• Perform comprehensive data entry including clinical survey scores/results, diagnostic tests, remote physiologic monitoring (RPM), and remote therapeutic monitoring (RTM) data streams.

• Schedule and coordinate patient appointments while maintaining communication with patients regarding their care schedule.

• Manage and process inbound and outbound medical records, ensuring proper documentation and organization.

• Maintain accurate EMR records by updating care notes, face sheets, and ensuring the integrity of patient information.

• Attend clinical meetings, take detailed notes, and follow up on action items and project timelines.

• Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of physicians, engineers, designers, and care tea
mbers to continuously improve patient care delivery.

• Resolve patient issues and complaints, identifying problems and finding appropriate solutions to ensure patient satisfaction while maintaining professional communication throughout the process.



The ideal candidate for this role at Ansible Health is a dynamic professional who thrives in a fast-paced, ever-changing startup environment. They should also possess exceptional English communication skills, both written and verbal, coupled with meticulous attention to detail and strong organizational abilities. They should be adept at managing multiple priorities, from patient onboarding to referral management.



Required Qualifications and Competencies

• Bachelor's degree in a relevant field, such as Nursing, Physical Therapy, or other healthcare-related fields.

• 2+ years of professional experience in a customer service or healthcare setting, demonstrating a deep understanding of patient care and care coordination.

• Solid grasp of the US healthcare system and its intricacies.

• Exceptional written and verbal English communication skills.

• Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.

• Proficiency with CRM systems, EMR software, and standard office applications (e.g., Google Suite)

• Training or experience in clinical medicine, such as a nursing degree, nursing assistant, or clinical scribe.

• Typing speed of at least 60 words per minute.

• Reliable internet connection (at least 30 Mbps) and a quiet home environment for work

• Ability to work in US time zones (EST)

• Achieve daily call quotas of 75-100 interactions.

• Adhere to established performance metrics to qualify for incentive plans tied to performance.



Nice to have

• Experience in the healthcare industry or with medical terminology.

• Proven track record in sales.

• Familiarity with remote patient monitoring systems.

• Knowledge of HIPAA regulations and healthcare privacy practices.



Job Details

Full-time: Full-time: 40 hours a week (with a possible shifting schedule including weekends)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work US hours - EST/EDT)

title: General Program Manager
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:





We are seeking a dynamic and talented General Program Manager to join our team. This role is perfect for a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare.



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We're seeking a super smart, hungry, and resilient individual who is passionate about technology and healthcare to join us as a Program Manager. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and brings a strong organizational mindset to complex projects. You will be responsible for overseeing multiple complex programs across various departments to ensure successful delivery within scope, timeline, and budget. This role requires a self-motivated individual who can work independently and adapt to the dynamic nature of a growing startup in the healthcare technology space.



Responsibilities

- Impact the strategy and execution for AnsibleHealth's general operations, clinical operations, and technology initiatives, working closely with cross-functional teams.

- Work independently on projects through the full life cycle, overseeing clinical, operational, and technology aspects.

- Develop and manage program plans, schedules, budgets, and resource allocation to ensure successful delivery.

- Define program scope, objectives, and success criteria in collaboration with stakeholders and cross-functional teams.

- Establish program governance structures and processes, including risk management, issue management, and change management.

- Monitor and report on program progress, status, and performance to stakeholders and executive leadership.

- Ensure effective communication and alignment with stakeholders at all levels.

- Continuously identify and mitigate program risks and issues.

- Facilitate program-related meetings and workshops.

- Develop and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders to drive program success and identify growth opportunities.

- Bring a continuous improvement mindset to our operations to ensure we're ready to scale.

- Anticipate issues, propose solutions, manage dependencies, and identify areas for tool/process improvement.

- Collaborate with and steer internal teams, including Marketing, Engineering, Program Management, and Clinical Operations.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable to be in a constantly changing role with a high level of ambiguity - as we are a fast-moving startup operating in an ever-evolving environment. He or she should have excellent analytical, communication and management skills to succeed in this role.



Education

Bachelor's degree from one of the top five universities in the Philippines.



Required Qualifications and competencies

- Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; 0-5 years of relevant work experience is an advantage but not required.

- Demonstrated passion for technology and ability to quickly adapt to new digital tools and platforms.

- Exceptional organizational skills.

- Resilient mindset with the ability to bounce back from challenges and thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

- Keen interest in healthcare and improving patient outcomes; prior healthcare experience is a plus but not mandatory.

- High intellectual curiosity and a hunger for personal and professional development.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically and creatively.

- Proficiency in Google Workspace and willingness to learn new software tools as needed.

- Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.

- Adaptable and comfortable with ambiguity in a startup environment.

- Eagerness to take on leadership roles and initiative to drive projects forward.

- Ability to influence and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.

- Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality work.

- Enthusiasm for continuous learning and process improvement.



The ideal candidate is a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare. We're looking for someone who can bring fresh perspectives, adapt quickly to new challenges, and contribute to our innovative and fast-paced work environment.



Job Details

Full-time: 40 hours a week, with flexible PTO and healthcare benefits.

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work Eastern hours)

title: Healthcare Technical Support
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/17b2a6df-a72d-480e-8c91-daa3830ac000



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

As a Technical Product Support Specialist, your primary responsibility is to provide patients with exceptional technical support for our products and services. You will daily engage non-tech-savvy patients on technical phone calls. Most of our patients are older adults located in the USA. These calls involve guiding patients through using their Android tablet and verifying it in the backend through Samsung Knox, ensuring that RPM devices are connected and transmitting accurate data, and assisting them in joining their appointments through our tablet via Zoom. Occasionally, patients may require additional help during their time in our program. In some instances, verifying data through Azure (Device Analytics and CosmosDB) may be necessary.



Furthermore, you will provide technical support to our clinical team, and ensure they are prepared for their virtual appointments. They may also require assistance using Athenahealth, such as modifying visits and checking in/out. You will handle general general technical support duties, which demand a solid understanding of our tech stack and products (i.e., Android Tablet, Zoo
etings and phone, Google Workspace). You should be capable of providing basic troubleshooting steps and assistance to remove obstacles for clinicians/support staff in their work, and are expected to be able to have on-call availability for urgent situations where patient care is affected.



Collaborating closely with our Engineering Team is also an essential aspect of your role to effectively escalate issues requiring broader coordination and resolution. GitLab and Azure DevOps are the primary ticketing systems used for filing issues and requests. Continuous follow-ups and monitoring are expected until the issue is resolved end-to-end. In addition, you are expected to be able to present and take notes on meetings about issues encountered in Tech.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable navigating ambiguity due to the ever-evolving nature of our startup. To excel in this role, excellent problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills are essential.



A Technical Product Support Specialist is expected to:

• Maintain a reliable, high-speed internet connection

• Handle Tech Support requests filed by clinicians or patients from end to end

• Manage and organize various project tools (e.g., GitLab, AzureDevOps, spreadsheets, bug trackers, documents)

• Conduct Tech Calls for non-tech-savvy patients (mainly older adults) in the USA

• Guide patients through Android tablet usage and confirm settings through Samsung Knox

• Work with RPM devices to ensure they are connected and transmitting accurate data

• Collaborate with Engineering Team and Program Managers to escalate and resolve issues

• Assist clinicians and company support staff with technical issues

• Run queries and check analytics using Azure’s CosmosDB or Device Analytics

• Become extremely familiar with our proprietary app, tablets, RPM devices, and backend systems.

• Support clinical team with equipment setup and checking applied security policies

• Present and take notes on Technical meetings, specifically on issues encountered and plans to resolve them.



Qualifications and Experience

• Prior experience in a customer support role, such as Technical Support Specialist or Support Engineer

• Expertise in Android Tablets, Samsung Knox Suite, and Knox Manage

• Proficiency in managing Zoo
etings and Zoom Phone

• Familiarity with medical devices (e.g., Blood Pressure monitor, Pulse Oximeter)

• Knowledge of Athenahealth preferred

• Experience using issue tracking software and remote support tools (e.g., Gitlab, Azure DevOps) is a plus

• Understanding of technology systems, particularly Google Workspace

• Positive, enthusiastic attitude and excellent English language skills (C2 level)

• Skilled in technical debugging techniques (e.g., analyzing metrics and logs on Azure)

• Strong written and verbal communication, problem-solving skills, and detail-oriented

• Ability to manage multiple priorities effectively

• Healthcare setting experience advantageous



Type of Role

Full-time: 40 hours a week (Monday - Friday; 9pm - 6pm EST/EDT)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage.

Level: Mid-Career, 3-5 years relevant work experience

Location: Remote

description:

title: HR Generalist
description:
***APPLICANTS WHO DO NOT FOLLOW INSTRUCTIONS WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY DISQUALIFIED***



We are looking for a skilled and experienced recruiter to manage various HR functions and ensure our HR initiatives are effective, efficient, and aligned with our business goals. The ideal candidate will have a passion for HR, excellent communication skills, and a keen eye for detail.



Key Responsibilities:



- Administer HR policies and procedures to ensure compliance and consistency.

- Manage recruitment processes, including job postings, interviews, and onboarding.

- Maintain accurate employee records and manage HR documentation.

- Assist in the development and implementation of HR strategies and initiatives.

- Conduct training sessions and support employee development programs.

- Ensure timely and accurate processing of payroll and benefits.

- Participate in performance management and employee evaluation processes.

- Provide support in other HR functions as needed.



Qualifications:



- Minimum of 4 years of experience in a Human Resources role (HR BPO experience is a plus)

- At least 6 months of experience working a graveyard shift.

- Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.

- Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

- Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.

- Proficiency in HR software and other tools

- Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently.

- A bachelor's degree in Human Resources or a related field is a plus.



Working Hours:



This is a remote position with a shift based in Eastern Standard Time (EST).

The position requires flexibility to work graveyard shifts as needed.



Benefits:



Competitive salary and benefits package.

Opportunities for professional growth and development.

Supportive and collaborative work environment.

Flexibility to work remotely.



How to Apply:

title: Real Estate Media Virtual Assistant (Photo QC + Media Delivery) | Photoshop / Lightroom
description:
Job Description



We are looking for a detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to help with quality control and delivery of real estate media.



This role is primarily responsible for reviewing real estate photos and videos before they are delivered to clients to ensure everything looks correct, complete, and professional.



You will review large batches of photos quickly and accurately, catching editing issues before delivery.

A typical shift may involve reviewing 100–200+ photos, so speed, focus, and attention to detail are extremely important.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys structured work, visual detail, and organized systems.

Compensation: $4–$8 USD/hour

Hours: 10–20 hours per week

Preferred schedule: Morning EST or CST



Your Main Responsibility (Quality Control)



This is the most important part of the role.



You will:

• Review 100+ real estate photos per shift

• Check for editing issues such as:

exposure problems

color balance issues

crooked vertical lines

cropping mistakes



HDR blending errors



object removal mistakes (trash cans, cords, etc.)

• Ensure photo order and folder structure are correct

• Confirm files are ready for delivery to clients

• Flag issues to the editing team when something needs fixing



You must be able to move through large batches of photos efficiently without missing mistakes.



Additional Responsibilities

File Organization

• Maintain organized Google Drive folders

• Upload and deliver final media



Instagram Outreach

• Send outreach messages to potential clients

• Track conversations and follow-ups



QC and media delivery are the core responsibilities, outreach is secondary.

Required Skills

Candidates must have real photo editing experience, specifically:

• Adobe Lightroom

• Adobe Photoshop



Important:

Canva experience does NOT count as Photoshop experience for this role.

You do not need to be a full editor, but you must be able to recognize when an image looks incorrect.

You should understand:



• exposure issues

• color balance

• vertical line correction

• cropping problems

• HDR blending issues



Major Bonus Experience

Experience with real estate media workflows is a major advantage.



Examples include:

• HDPhotoHub

• Spiro

• AutoHDR

• Real estate media delivery platforms

• HDR editing workflows



Candidates with these skills usually ramp up much faster.



Ideal Candidate Background



The best candidates often come from:

• Photo editors

• Real estate media virtual assistants

• Real estate marketing assistants

• Photo retouchers

• Post-production assistants



Traits We Look For



You are likely a great fit if you are:

• Extremely detail oriented

• Comfortable reviewing large batches of images quickly

• Organized and process-driven

• Able to work independently after training

• Reliable and consistent with your schedule

• Comfortable communicating when issues arise



Non-Negotiables

• Availability: 10–20 hours per week

• Morning availability (EST/CST preferred)

• Reliable internet connection (large files involved)

• Clear written English

• Photoshop and Lightroom familiarity

• Ownership mindset



How to Apply



Please include the following in your application:



A brief introduction about your experience with Photoshop and Lightroom



Your internet speed



Links to any photo editing work or portfolio



Your availability (days and hours)



To confirm you read this entire job post, please include the phrase:

“I care about details.”

at the top of your application.

Applications that do not include this phrase will not be considered.

title: 3D Designer / Motion & UI/UX Designer
description:
3D Designer / Motion & UI Designer



To apply, view the full task brief and submit your work: 



What is the company?

- We partner with influencers to build products with them.

- We're a London-based startup redefining how creators build real tech companies. Our team handles the product, design, tech, and growth while influencers bring the audience and creative spark.

- Every project is built like a startup: fast-moving, ambitious, and impact-focused.



What will you be doing?

- You'll be our first core 3D designer hire, working directly with the founders and developers who've built products used by millions.

- You'll take ownership of character design, 3D motion, and UI/UX across multiple projects, from stylised 3D characters and equippable items to celebration emotes and the interfaces they live inside.

- This role is perfect for someone who can make a product feel alive through expressive 3D characters and polished UI, all while - keeping things lightweight and ready to ship on mobile.



Responsibilities

- Design and model stylised 3D characters to a Fortnite / Fall Guys standard that are optimised for mobile apps.

- Create equippable items and consumables that layer cleanly onto base characters.

- Produce expressive emote and celebration animations with personality and polish.

- Design beautiful, intuitive UI/UX for mobile apps and websites, integrating 3D assets seamlessly into product interfaces.

- Collaborate with founders, developers, and creators to turn ideas into interactive, user-ready designs.

- Build clean design systems and component libraries for consistent scaling.

- Provide creative direction on visual identity, character design, and motion style.

- Iterate quickly based on feedback.



About you

- You're ambitious, creative, and obsessed with details. You love creating characters and interfaces that don't just look good, they feel good.

- You thrive in a fast-paced startup environment where autonomy, execution, and taste matter.



You should be:

- Highly skilled in 3D character modelling and texturing with a stylised, game-ready aesthetic (Blender, Cinema 4D, Maya, or similar).

- Experienced in 3D animation, rigging, and motion design (emotes, idle loops, celebration sequences).

- Strong with UI/UX design for apps and web (Figma proficiency required).

- Capable of delivering lightweight, optimised 3D assets suitable for mobile (clean topology, efficient poly counts, compressed textures).

- Able to take loose ideas and turn them into finished, polished characters and interfaces.

- Organised, a clear communicator, and reliable with deadlines.

- Passionate about creators, gaming aesthetics, design, and tech.



What you'll get

- The chance to design characters and products that reach millions.

- Direct collaboration with founders and creators shaping the next generation of consumer brands.

- A fast-growing, creative environment with real ownership and freedom.

- Exposure to multiple products across industries (AI, education, fragrance, fitness, and more).



Payment and Terms

- Base: $1,500 USD per month (this is negotiable along with performance-based bonuses when deadlines are hit).

- Hours: 40 hours per week (exclusive of breaks).

- Weekends: Off.

- Holidays: Paid (dates flexible).



How to apply



View the full task brief and submit your completed work to 



Please include your portfolio and resume with your submission so we can see your previous work.







We'll respond to successful applicants within 7 days of submission.



Best character + best UI wins. You start immediately.

title: VA
description:
About the Company



KBR Rentals LLC is a fast-growing car rental and rent-to-own company based in Houston, Texas, specializing in providing vehicles to rideshare and gig economy drivers. We’re seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Virtual Assistant (VA) to join our remote operations team.



?



Position Overview



The VA will play a key role in sales, customer service, and administrative support — helping manage daily operations, qualify incoming leads, handle invoicing, coordinate vehicle scheduling, and assist with problem-solving tasks related to fleet management and customer relations.



This position is ideal for someone who’s professional, quick to learn, confident in communication, and able to multitask in a fast-paced environment.



?



Key Responsibilities

 • Lead Qualification & Sales Support

 • Respond to and qualify leads from
, website forms, and SMS/CRM pipelines.

 • Follow scripts and guidelines to vet potential renters.

 • Schedule appointments and maintain follow-ups through CRM (GoHighLevel).

 • Customer Service & Communication

 • Handle client questions, complaints, and inquiries professionally.

 • Coordinate vehicle pickup and return schedules.

 • Communicate with drivers about payments, due dates, and maintenance updates.

 • Invoicing & Admin Tasks

 • Create and send QuickBooks invoices to customers.

 • Track payments, late fees, and balances in company spreadsheets.

 • Maintain accurate customer records and communication logs.

 • Operations & Research

 • Assist in researching parts, shops, and service providers for vehicle maintenance.

 • Follow up on repair statuses, shop invoices, and vendor quotes.

 • Track oil changes, tire rotations, and maintenance schedules.

 • Documentation & File Management

 • Update contracts, edit forms, and organize digital documents.

 • Assist in designing flyers or editing documents using Adobe Photoshop, Canva, or similar tools.

 • Ensure all updates are properly stored in Google Drive or CRM.



?



Qualifications

 • Excellent English communication skills (written & spoken).

 • Previous experience as a VA, Customer Service Rep, or Operations Assistant preferred.

 • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

 • Comfortable handling invoicing, spreadsheets, and CRM systems.

 • Proficient with:

 • QuickBooks Online

 • Google Sheets / Excel

 • Canva or Adobe Photoshop

 • GoHighLevel or similar CRM tools

 • Able to multitask, solve problems quickly, and handle customer concerns calmly.

 • Reliable internet connection and consistent availability during U.S. business hours.



?



Preferred Skills (Bonus Points)

 • Experience with car rental, logistics, or transportation industries.

 • Understanding of billing workflows and payment collection.

 • Familiarity with using GPS tracking software or fleet management tools.



?



Compensation & Benefits

 • Competitive hourly rate (based on experience)

 • Monthly performance bonuses

 • Long-term growth opportunity within a scaling U.S.-based company

 • Stable and consistent workload with training provided



How to Apply

 • Submit a 1-minute video (no more, no less) answering:

title: TIKTOK SHOP EXPERT (Full-Stack Management & Growth)

• Logistics and FBT: Monitor daily orders to ensure they meet TikTok’s strict On-Time Delivery and Late Dispatch metrics (tracking 2026 shipping requirements), manage FBT shipments by allocating addresses for product inbound and ensuring seamless warehouse integration.

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
Remote Sales Development Representative

Kelly Agency: Allstate – Pottstown, PA



Our challenge:

The Kelly Agency, a leading Allstate Insurance Agency, is looking for a dynamic Remote Sales Development

Representative to join our Outside Sales Team. Earn a 240 PHP base wage plus daily and monthly bonuses, with

top performers averaging 10,000+ PHP/month in bonuses.



Who we are:

At the Kelly Agency, we are driven by passion and teamwork, committed to building strong personal

relationships. As one of Pennsylvania’s largest remote-friendly Allstate Agencies, we rank in the top 10%

nationwide for new business growth and customer satisfaction. Our team thrives on shared incentives and

continuous improvement, all while maintaining a healthy work-life balance. Embracing cutting-edge technology,

we utilize digital tools such as social media, texting, video, online quoting, and lead management software to

enhance our operations.

We take immense pride in our customer experience, safeguarding what matters most to our clients and their

families. Our core values are: putting customers first, staying positive, being honest, embracing growth, and

working together.



Follow us on Social 

Visit our Website: 



The job description:

Insurance is essential, yet often confusing. As a Sales Development Representative, you'll provide a friendly,

reliable, and informative experience to prospective customers, by connecting with them on the phone and

introducing them to our licensed agents for quoting. This entry-level position offers a career path within our

qualifiers.

Some benefits we provide:

Join us at the Kelly Agency and be a part of a forward-thinking, tech-driven team dedicated to customer

excellence and personal growth.

title: We're Hiring: Facebook Ads Media Buyer
description:
Meta Ads Media Buyer — Marketing Agency | Remote | $1,500–$2,000/month



What We Do



We run Meta ads for outdoor living contractors (pergolas, pools, turf, pavers, outdoor kitchens) across the US. Our model is simple: we generate leads, our setters book appointments, contractors close deals. We charge per appointment. If the ads don't perform, clients leave. If they perform, clients stay and grow. The media buyer is the most important role in our company.



What You'll Own



You will manage 15–25 Meta ad accounts independently. That means:



Campaign strategy, setup, and optimization across all accounts. You d
 what to test, when to scale, when to kill. Nobody is going to tell you what buttons to press.



Creative direction: You write briefs for our video editor, review output, and d
 what runs. You don't need to edit video, but you need to know what converts.



Cost per lead and cost per appointment are your numbers. You own them. If CPL is high, you fix it. You don't wait for someone to tell you there's a problem.



Landing page and lead form optimization. You'll work inside GoHighLevel (GHL) to build and test funnels. If you haven't used GHL, that's fine. But you need to understand funnel logic.



Who This Is For



You've managed Meta campaigns for local service businesses. not ecom, not info products, not dropshipping. Local. You understand targeting, lead forms vs landing pages, and why cost per lead alone is a bad metric without conversion data behind it.



You can look at an ad account and within 10 minutes tell me what's wrong and what you'd test first. Not because you read it in a course.



You don't need to be managed. You need to be pointed in a direction and left alone to produce results. If you need daily check ins and hand holding, this is not the role.



Who This Is NOT For

If you completed a Meta ads course and now call yourself a media buyer, don't apply!



If your experience is boosting posts for restaurants, don't apply!



If you need someone to review every decision before you make it, don't apply!



Compensation

$1,500–$2,000/month depending on experience. This is a full-time remote position. We're a small team that communicates on Slack. We don't do daily standups or unnecessary meetings.



We do results.



Apply here: 



We care if you can think independently and deliver results. If that's you, apply. If you're not sure, it's probably not.

description:
Do you want to create visual assets that will help transform the careers of millions of people worldwide?



We're looking for a freelance Canva infographics / carousel / poster guru (digital asset creator and social media support) to help develop engaging, high-impact LinkedIn posts for one of the most influential career advice brands on the Internet.



Key Responsibility:

Social Media Asset Creation

• Create media graphics (using Canva) for LinkedIn such as carousels, infographics, and single images.

• Follow digital branding requirements including logos, text, images, and layout to create a polished, professional final product.

• Optimize content and sizing for LinkedIn’s algorithm (including mobile format).



BONUS opportunity for:

Social Media Support

• Respond to user comments and tags using our standard operating procedures.

• Conduct outreach to relevant users according to the provided criteria.

• Respond to inbound direct messages to our inbox according to the provided criteria.

• Conduct outbound comments and outbound messages according to the provided criteria.



Requirements:

• Proven experience in social media asset design.

• Familiarity with the LinkedIn platform including best optimization practices.

• Familiarity with the Canva platform including best practices for LinkedIn assets.

• Attention to detail—we expect strong proofreading skills and polished copy.

• Willing to write according to a brand voice (by hand and not just with AI).

• Excellent command of written English.



Preference for candidates who can work in Eastern Time (USA) though all candidates will be considered.



Additional Information:



• You will receive style guides and previous posts to help you align with our brand voice.

• A short graphic creation test will be required as part of the application process.

• If applicable: A short social media management test will be required as part of the application process.

• Long-term opportunity to increase your hours, increase your pay, and become a bigger part of the team long-term.



To Apply, You Must:

1) Apply here on OLJ using *5* Apply Points.

2) Fill out this Google Form: 
 

Applications that do not complete items 1 & 2 will not be considered.



If you move to the next steps, you will be asked to do a test trial where you will be asked to create digital graphic assets to accompany an existing LinkedIn post. Please note that the test trial is paid (fixed payment of $5 with a 72-hour turnaround time). You will receive more details via
 if you have been selected to do a test trial. A test trial is not a guarantee of an interview or further work.



We review applications on a rolling basis and will contact you to do a paid test trial within 48 hours of your application.

title: Senior Automation Engineer (Video Systems & Workflow Automation)
description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing UGC agency that produces high-volume short-form content for startup apps using creator networks. We operate at scale and rely heavily on systems, automation, and internal tooling.



We are building proprietary software to manage video workflows, editing pipelines, scheduling systems, and large-scale content operations.



We are looking for a serious automation engineer to help us architect and build this infrastructure.



What You’ll Be Building



Automated video processing pipelines



Native-style short-form video editing systems



Content versioning and bulk rendering systems



Account management dashboards



Scheduling and distribution workflows



Internal tools to manage creator submissions and publishing



You will help us design scalable systems that can handle very high daily content volume.



Responsibilities



Design scalable backend systems for video automation



Build tools that process and modify short-form videos at scale



Integrate with official APIs and third-party services where applicable



Build internal dashboards for managing content operations



Optimize performance for high-volume workflows



Ensure system reliability and fault tolerance



Document architecture clearly



Requirements



Strong experience with Python or Node.js



Experience with video processing libraries (FFmpeg, etc.)



Experience building automation systems



Strong understanding of APIs and system architecture



Database experience (SQL / NoSQL)



Experience deploying to cloud environments



Strong English communication



Nice to Have



Experience with high-volume media systems



Experience building SaaS tools



Experience with workflow automation engines



Experience with queue systems (Redis, RabbitMQ, etc.)



Important



We are not looking for basic script builders.

We are building long-term internal infrastructure and need someone who understands scale, architecture, and reliability.



To Apply



Please include:



Examples of automation systems you’ve built



Any experience with media processing



GitHub or portfolio



Your monthly rate



Your availability

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer & Automation Enginee
description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing digital agency building internal systems to scale operations, outreach, content workflows, and data infrastructure. We move fast and rely heavily on automation to operate efficiently.



We are looking for a high-level full-stack developer who can take ideas and turn them into fully functional systems.



Role Overview



We need someone who can build and automate almost anything we conceptualize.



If we describe a workflow, you should be able to:



Design the architecture



Choose the right tech stack



Build it



Deploy it



Maintain and optimize it



This is a systems-builder role, not a task-based coding job.



What You’ll Be Building



Custom web applications and dashboards



Backend systems and APIs



Workflow automation tools



Data scraping and processing systems



CRM and internal management tools



API integrations between multiple platforms



Automation pipelines to reduce manual labor



Responsibilities



Architect scalable systems from scratch



Build full-stack applications (frontend + backend)



Automate repetitive workflows



Integrate third-party APIs



Optimize and maintain existing tools



Ensure performance, stability, and clean documentation



Proactively suggest better technical solutions



Requirements



Strong experience in full-stack development



Proficiency in Python, Node.js, or similar backend languages



Strong frontend skills (React, Vue, or similar)



Database experience (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, etc.)



Experience building automation systems



Experience deploying to cloud environments



Strong problem-solving ability



Excellent English communication



Nice to Have



Experience building SaaS platforms



Experience with workflow engines and queue systems



Experience with server architecture and scaling



Experience with system security best practices



What We’re Looking For



Someone who thinks like an engineer, not just a coder



Someone who can translate business ideas into technical systems



Someone long-term who wants to grow with the company



To Apply



Please include:



A short introduction



Links to projects or GitHub



Examples of automation systems you’ve built



Your monthly rate



Your availability

title: Podcast Content Producer
description:
We are looking for a Content Producer to join our team and help us create engaging, well-researched, and professional podcast content. The Raising Men Podcast is about building a brotherhood of intentional fathers committed to raising sons of character, courage, and conviction. 



Responsibilities:

1. Topic Research – Conduct in-depth research with novelty and edge, preparing a research doc (facts, stats, sources, edge cases).

2. Host Prep – Write host intros, guest bios, and a structured question bank with optional follow-ups.

3. Episode Outline – Draft run-of-show documents (cold open, beats, pivots, plug, CTA).

4. Show Notes – From a long-form video, create show notes, identify quotes, and research & link guests.

5. Social Media Content – Write and create posts that repurpose episode content.

6. Publishing – Upload on YouTube (with chapter markers) and schedule posts on social media platforms.



Requirements:

1. Experience writing for podcasts, blogs, or long-form content is a big plus.

2. Excellent English writing & communication skills.

3. Ability to structure conversations, scripts, and show outlines.

4. Familiarity with YouTube uploading, SEO, and social media management.

5. Highly organized and detail-oriented.



To apply, please reply with:

1. A link to your resume (showing past company/work).

2. A link to your portfolio (content writing, research, or podcast-related work).

3. Your hourly rate.

4. Your availability date and number of hours available per week.

title: Podcast Video Editor
description:
We are looking for a skilled Podcast Video & Audio Editor to join us on a part-time or as-needed basis. Our objective is to build a brotherhood of intentional fathers committed to raising sons of character, courage, and conviction. Our tone is authoritative, educational, and conversational.



Responsibilities:

1. Intro/Outro Animations: Animate the podcast logo for opening (OBB) and closing (CBB). [One-time]

2. Edit from a long-form recording into a polished episode.

3. Add name tags / lower thirds.

4. Insert smooth transitions, subtle sound effects, and/or background music.

5. Enhance and synchronize audio for clear, professional quality.

6. Weekly tea
eting



Deliverables per episode:

1. Long-form audio file (podcast-ready).

2. Long-form video file with subtitles (YouTube).

3. Multiple vertical short-form clips (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts).

4. Brand Alignment: Ensure edits reflect the podcast’s niche: raising men, authority, education, conversation.



Requirements

- Proven experience editing podcasts or similar long-form content.

- Strong skills in Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, or similar.

- Familiarity with audio enhancement (noise reduction, leveling, EQ).

- Knowledge of social media formats (TikTok, IG Reels, YouTube Shorts).

- Strong eye for storytelling pacing, typography, and visual polish.



To be considered, please reply with:

1. A link to your resume (showing past companies/work).

2. A link to your portfolio (showing podcast or similar video editing projects).

3. Your hourly rate.

4. Your availability (part-time hours per week or as-needed basis).

title: Virtual Assistant for Online Business (Social Media, Etsy, Google Docs, Systems Support)
description:
I run an online coaching and education company that supports salon and spa owners in building profitable beauty businesses. Our company creates digital products, online trainings, podcast content, and educational resources for beauty professionals.



I am looking for a Virtual Assistant who already has experience working inside online business systems and can help manage several areas of the business behind the scenes.



This is not a basic assistant role. I am specifically looking for someone who is already familiar with platforms like social media scheduling tools, Etsy listings, and Google Workspace so they can step in and help manage these areas without needing extensive training.



Many of the systems inside the business are already created, but I need someone who can help maintain them, organize them, and implement tasks inside those platforms.



Some of the areas you would be helping with include:



• Managing and scheduling social media posts

• Uploading and organizing digital products on Etsy

• Updating and maintaining product listings

• Organizing Google Docs and Google Sheets

• Managing spreadsheets and trackers

• Uploading and organizing content across platforms

• Assisting with digital product management

• Helping keep systems organized and running smoothly



Because much of this work happens inside platforms I may not personally manage day-to-day, I am specifically looking for someone who already has experience working inside these types of systems and can confidently navigate them.



The right person for this role is someone who is:



• Highly organized

• Comfortable working independently

• Experienced with online tools and digital platforms

• Detail oriented

• Able to follow instructions while also solving small problems independently



This position is best suited for someone who enjoys helping keep an online business organized and operating smoothly behind the scenes.



To confirm you read this entire post, please include the word “SYSTEMS” at the top of your application.

title: Application For Bookkeeper Virtual Assistant (with experience in Adaptive)
description:
Bond Business Services is hiring a Bookkeeper Virtual Assistant with experience in QuickBooks Online and construction bookkeeping. This role supports a growing bookkeeping firm that helps small businesses stay financially organized and confident in their numbers.



The primary focus of this position is managing bookkeeping for a construction client using Adaptive software connected with QuickBooks Online, along with maintaining books for 5–10 smaller QuickBooks Online clients.



This position suits someone who takes initiative, stays organized, and looks for ways to improve processes when work is complete. You will work closely with the business owner and help build systems that keep the firm efficient as it grows.





Key Responsibilities:



1. Client Bookkeeping Management

- Maintain accurate financial records for multiple clients using QuickBooks Online

- Categorize transactions and reconcile accounts

- Keep client books clean and organized



2. Construction Accounting Support

- Assist a construction client using Adaptive software integrated with QuickBooks Online

- Maintain organized financial records for construction accounting workflows



3. Client Onboarding & Admin

- Help prepare onboarding
 templates and document checklists

- Assist with collecting documents needed from new clients

- Document processes and internal workflows

- Help improve organization inside the business

- Look for ways to improve efficiency and support growth



Requirements:

- Accounting degree or CPA equivalent preferred

- 2+ years QuickBooks Online experience required

- Construction bookkeeping experience preferred

- Adaptive software experience

- Fluent English communication (spoken and written)

- Strong attention to detail

- Excellent organization and time management skills

- Ability to manage multiple client accounts

- Strong problem-solving mindset

- Ability to work independently



What We Offer:

- Competitive salary

- Direct US company

- Free training and development opportunities

- Yearly salary reviews

- Supportive and collaborative tea
mbers

- Positive and service-focused work culture

- A professional team that values reliability, initiative, and improvement



We're hiring a Virtual Assistant Bookkeeper for a long-term position with a knack for handling dynamic schedules, juggling multiple priorities, and staying two steps ahead. If you're not interested in a lasting role with growth opportunities and you don't have the qualifications, please do not apply.



But if this resonates with you, we look forward to your application form!

title: Digital Growth Operator – Video Ads & Paid Media
description:
We're a bathroom renovation company in Scotland and we're looking for one exceptional person — not a average freelancer, not someone who "knows a bit of everything" — someone who is genuinely outstanding at what they do online.

This is a results-driven role. You will own our digital presence, create video ads that actually stop people scrolling, and run Meta and Google ad campaigns that bring in real leads. If you need to be taught the basics, this is not for you.



Be honest with yourself before applying:



Can you produce video ads that look genuinely professional — not just slapped together on CapCut with a template?

Have you actually run Meta or Google ad campaigns and got real results — not just boosted a post?

Can you speak and write in excellent English and occasionally call a customer confidently?

Are you the type of person who figures things out themselves rather than asking what to do next?





If you answered yes to all of the above — we want to hear from you. If you're still learning, please do not apply. We've been through too many applicants who oversell themselves and underdeliver.



What you'll be doing:



Creating high-quality, professional video ads for our bathroom renovation projects

Running and optimising Meta Ads and Google Ads campaigns to generate leads

Owning performance — you'll know if it's working or not and act on it

Occasional customer calls in fluent English

General digital tasks as the business grows — we need someone who can adapt



What we offer:



$600-$1200/month base salary + 20% Real performance bonus when monthly targets are hit — this is where sharp people earn significantly more

A long-term role with growing responsibility as you prove yourself

Creative freedom — we trust results, not micromanagement



WORKING HOURS

9:00am – 5:00pm UK Time or 5:00pm – 1:00am Philippine Time (shift to be agreed upon hire)



To apply: Send a short introduction, your portfolio of video ad work, and at least one example of a Meta or Google Ads campaign you have managed with results. Applications without proof of work will be ignored.

title: Executive Assistant with Social Media manager
description:
About Our Ministry

We are a growing, Spirit-filled church based in Tampa, Florida — passionate about reaching our community and the world through the power of the Gospel. We are expanding our digital presence and need a sharp, motivated Online Executive Assistant to help our Pastor stay organized, reach more people online, and create compelling content that drives real engagement and ministry growth.



Role Overview

We're looking for a highly organized, creative, and tech-savvy individual who can wear multiple hats. You will serve as the right hand to our Pastor — managing his schedule, leading social media content production, coordinating with our video editing team, and responding to fast-moving ministry needs. This is more than a job; it's an opportunity to help build something meaningful.



Key Responsibilities

Scheduling & Calendar Management

Manage and maintain the Pastor's personal and ministry calendar

Schedule and confirm appointments, meetings, and ministry engagements

Send timely reminders and follow-ups to keep the Pastor on track

Coordinate logistics for events, speaking engagements, and personal commitments



Social Media Content Creation & Posting

Create and post cutting-edge, daily content on TikTok, Instagram, and

Repurpose existing YouTube videos and sermon notes into platform-optimized content

Design eye-catching graphics using photos, scripture notes, and sermon highlights

Develop short-form video clips (Reels, TikToks) from full-length YouTube content

Build and execute a consistent daily posting schedule to increase reach and engagement

Monitor performance metrics and adjust content strategy for maximum growth and traction



YouTube & Video Coordination

Upload and optimize existing YouTube content for search and discovery

Write compelling titles, descriptions, and tags for all video content

Communicate and coordinate directly with the video editing team on deliverables

Track video performance and identify high-performing content to repurpose



Communications & Personal Support

Respond quickly to incoming messages,
s, inquiries, and ministry needs on behalf of the Pastor

Manage
, DMs, and other communication channels professionally

Serve as a point of contact for church members, partners, and visitors online

Handle ad hoc personal scheduling and administrative tasks as needed



What We're Looking For

Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience managing social media accounts (TikTok, Instagram,
, YouTube)

Strong content creation skills — writing, graphic design, and short-form video editing

Experience with calendar and scheduling tools (Google Calendar, Calendly, or similar)

Excellent written communication skills with a professional, warm tone

Self-motivated with strong ability to manage tasks independently and meet deadlines

Highly responsive — able to handle urgent requests quickly and efficiently



Preferred / Bonus Skills

Familiarity with church culture and ministry environments

Experience with Canva, Adobe Express, CapCut, or similar design/editing tools

Video editing experience or ability to create basic cuts/clips from longer content

Knowledge of social media growth strategies and content algorithms

Experience working with or supporting a public figure, executive, or ministry leader



What You'll Gain

A front-row seat to a growing ministry making a real impact in Tampa and beyond

Creative freedom to build and shape the church's digital brand and online presence

Flexible, remote work environment with a supportive and faith-filled team

Hands-on experience in ministry media, content strategy, and digital evangelism

Competitive compensation based on experience and hours committed



You're the right fit if you...

Love social media and stay on top of trends — especially on TikTok and Instagram

Are highly organized and take pride in keeping things running smoothly

Are passionate about faith, community, and making a difference

Thrive in fast-paced environments and respond quickly under pressure

Can take raw ideas — sermon notes, YouTube videos, photos — and turn them into content that moves people

title: Hospitality Phone & Guest Services Coordinator (Remote / Full?Time)
description:
