Applicants must be willing and able to work during New York–based restaurant hours.



We are a luxury hospitality group based in Long Island, New York looking for a calm, articulate, and service?oriented professional to become the first voice our guests hear.

This role is critical to our brand. You are not a call center — you are a guest ambassador.



Responsibilities:



- Answer all incoming calls for multiple restaurants

- Assist guests with:

1. Reservations & dining questions

2. Hours, menus, and events

3. Gift cards and special requests

- Route urgent matters correctly

- Log guest issues and missed calls

- Maintain a warm, professional, unhurried tone



Requirements:

Excellent spoken English (neutral accent preferred)

Hospitality or customer service experience

Calm under pressure

Highly organized and reliable

Quiet workspace + stable internet



We Are Looking For Someone Who:

Enjoys helping people

Takes pride in professionalism

Understands that tone matters

Wants long?term work

title: Virtual Assistant/Social Media Expert
description:
Now Hiring: Virtual Assistant (Operations, Marketing & Admin Support)



We are seeking a highly reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to help manage and grow our multi-functional academy. This role supports three connected businesses:

 • Marlow’s Best – Summer Camp & After-School Program

 • Marlow Martial Arts Academy

 • Marlow Batting Club



This is a remote position ideal for someone who is organized, proactive, and looking for a long-term opportunity with room to grow.





Position Overview



You will work directly with the owner to support daily operations, communication, marketing, and administrative tasks across all three brands.



High-speed, reliable internet is required.



We are looking for someone who:

 • Is extremely detail-oriented and organized

 • Speaks and writes English fluently

 • Is dependable, responsive, and consistent

 • Is a self-starter who takes initiative

 • Is willing to learn, be trained, and improve systems

 • Is committed to a long-term working relationship and growing with the company



Hours & Pay

 • Pay: Up to $5.00 per hour

 • Hours: 10-20 hours per week to start

 • Growth Opportunity: Additional hours and responsibility available based on performance

 • Probationary Period:

This position includes a 6-week probationary period to ensure a strong mutual fit.



Responsibilities (Including but not limited to)



Marketing & Social Media

 • Create and schedule 2 social media posts daily for 3 business pages on IG, FB, TikTok and Youtube

 • Assist with content planning, promotions, and campaigns

 • Design graphics and flyers (Canva or similar)

 • Edit short-form videos for Reels, promos, and ads



Lead & Client Communication

 • Manage and follow up on leads and inquiries

 • Reply to calls, texts, DMs, and messages professionally

 • Respond to
s and maintain a clean, organized inbox

 • Label, archive, and flag communications appropriately



Admin, Tracking & Organization

 • Track stats and performance using spreadsheets

 • Maintain organized systems, files, and workflows

 • Assist with planning and marketing future events



Retail & Merch

 • Set up and manage an online retail/merch store

 • Update products, descriptions, pricing, and inventory as needed



Bookkeeping Support

 • Assist with basic bookkeeping tasks

 • Organize receipts and transactions

 • Categorize income and expenses

 • Prepare records for accountant review (QuickBooks or similar)



Required Assessments

 • DISC Personality Profile required

(Candidates must submit results or be willing to complete a DISC assessment during the hiring process.)



Ideal Candidate Traits

 • Reliable and consistent

 • Strong attention to detail

 • Open to feedback and coaching

 • Comfortable juggling multiple tasks and brands

 • Invested in learning and improving over time



How to Apply



Please submit:

 1. Your resume

 2. A 1–2 minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you’re a great fit

 3. Your DISC personality profile (or confirmation you are willing to complete one)



We are not looking for short-term freelancers. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with our company, take ownership of their role, and be part of a long-term team.

title: Tier 2 Customer Support - API Specialist
description:
As a Tier 2 Customer Support - API Specialist, you will ensure our customers are set up for success by answering product questions, advising best practices, and assisting with any technical hurdles related to API functionality. You will also influence our product design and direction by relaying customer requests back to the management teams. If you enjoy working with people and geek out about the technical and business aspects of the solar industry, we would love to hear from you!



In your application, please start your sentence with "What a Solar day!"



Please DO NOT apply if you do not have experience in API integration and scripting languages such as Python, JavaScript, or C++. 





WHAT YOU'LL DO:



Becoming an expert on the Solar software, including its usage, integrations, and API functionality



Assist customers in troubleshooting issues they encounter when building integrations with the software's API



Reply to inbound customer inquiries and questions through various channels, including chat,
, phone, and virtual meetings



Handle escalated technical and operational issues from Tier 1 that require deeper troubleshooting



Identify, troubleshoot, and escalate bugs to the development team using standardized escalation paths



Document solutions, troubleshooting steps, and process improvements for future reference



Contributing to internal and client-facing documentation about the platform and its usage



Collaborate with Tier 3, Engineering, or other teams for advanced problem resolution



Perform initial quality assurance check on component inputs, and run component uploads





WHAT WE VALUE:



Experience utilizing and troubleshooting API requests,s including authentication, sending and receiving data, and how said data can be consumed by downstream systems



Experience with scripting languages such as Python, JavaScript, or C++



Excellent written and verbal communication skills



Exceptional troubleshooting and problem-solving skills



2 years or more of experience in a technical, client-facing B2B SaaS role



Demonstrated interest in solar technology



Passion for improving procedures, documentation, and efficiency



Knowledge of JIRA or Zendesk



Experience in a startup environment



Knowledge of solar finance options is a plus

title: Social Media Content Creator + Admin Assistant
description:
Social Media Content Creator + Operations Assistant

(Canva + Short-Form Video + Organized Execution)



We are hiring a creative, organized assistant to help produce content and keep day-to-day operations moving inside a growing child development and parenting brand.



This is not a typical VA role.



Part of this role is creating social media content, and part of the role is keeping projects, messages, and deadlines organized so nothing slips through the cracks.



The pace is real. We move fast. The work matters.



If you enjoy creating clean, engaging content and also take pride in being extremely organized, this will feel like a natural fit.



If you only want admin work or only want design work, this role probably isn’t right for you.



What You Will Do



Content Creation



• Create social media graphics in Canva

• Repurpose existing content into clean, engaging posts

• Edit short-form clips using CapCut or similar tools

• Organize and schedule social media content

• Assist with a monthly layout project in Adobe InDesign



Operations Support



• Manage and organize the calendar

• Manage inbox flow and flag priorities

• Keep the CRM clean and updated

• Track deadlines so projects stay on schedule

• Support day-to-day operational tasks as needed



Skills Required



• Confident using Canva

• Comfortable editing short-form video (CapCut or similar)

• Strong written English

• Highly organized and detail-oriented

• Reliable internet connection



Nice to Have



• Adobe InDesign experience

• Experience supporting a content creator, coach, or online brand



Time Requirements



At least 50% of your working hours must overlap with Eastern Time (New York).



Compensation



$5–6 per hour depending on experience.



We are looking for someone long-term who wants to grow with a fast-moving brand.



How to Apply



Please send the following:



1.) A short video introduction about yourself



2.) Two Canva graphics that you personally designed



3.) One short-form video you edited (CapCut or similar)



4.) Your desired hourly rate



5.) Your availability and EST working hours



Somewhere in your message include the word AWESOME so we know you read the full description.



Applications without examples of your work will not be reviewed.



We’re looking for someone who enjoys turning ideas into content, staying organized, and helping a growing brand move forward every single week.

 (automation platform)

Our content should feel:

We are looking for someone who can grow with our company and help us build a modern, efficient social media content system.



Please include the word “Comet” in the first line of your application so we know you read the full description.

title: GoHighLevel CRM & Workflow Specialist – Wellness Coaching & Treatment Practice
description:
We’ve already built most of the core structure in the backend — pipelines, workflows, tags, and custom fields are all in place. But now that we’re getting into more advanced areas (like coaching, treatments, and product flows), it’s becoming more technical and time-consuming to manage.

That’s why we’re now looking for someone like you to take over the system — not just to fix what’s needed, but to lead and manage the CRM as we continue to grow.

? What We’re Hoping You Can Take Over:

Troubleshooting and fixing the treatment workflow (details below)





Reviewing and simplifying the current setup where needed





Finalizing and polishing all client-facing automations





Reorganizing or reworking parts of the backend if it makes the system more efficient





Continuing to build out workflows as we expand programs, services, and offers





Making the system easier for our team to manage and maintain over time





You can absolutely redo or improve anything we’ve already built if you see a better way to handle it. We welcome your ideas and trust your experience. The goal is to make sure the CRM is clean, strategic, and fully functional — now and long-term.

???? Current System Status

Consultation Workflow is live and working: Bookings through GHL or Calendly trigger the correct reminders and follow-ups.





Appointment Feedback Form is still a draft: We haven’t finalized the logic yet for segmenting by service type (coaching, treatments, products).





Treatment Workflow is built, but it’s not firing correctly when multiple treatments are selected.





???? Main Issue: Treatment Workflow

The workflow is supposed to:

Apply tags for each treatment selected





Update the custom fields (selected_treatments, treatment_details)





Send follow-up
s based on those selections





But in testing (e.g., Functional Cranial Release, ProstaBlueZone, TRT/StemWave), only one treatment shows in the
, even when multiple were selected.

Potential Causes:

“Stop on Previous” is active





Field data may be overwriting, not appending





Some tags may not be triggering





Merge fields may have formatting issues





This is the top priority we’d love you to address first.

We're Open to Your Strategy

We’re handing the CRM over to you — and we want you to take the lead on improving, optimizing, and expanding it. If you want to restructure parts, rename elements, or rebuild certain workflows in a more efficient way, we fully support that. We trust your judgment and we’re happy to collaborate as needed.





























Current CRM Setup Overview – To Be Continued and Improved

This is the current and temporary CRM setup we’ve built so far. It’s a working draft, but not yet complete — and we’re currently stuck on the treatment workflow, mainly because of the number of treatment types and the complexity involved in branching logic.

You’ll be continuing from here — reviewing, improving, and finalizing this structure. The ultimate goal is to guide every lead smoothly through the journey until they either:

Confirm they want to work with Tara (coaching)





Purchase a product





Schedule or commit to a treatment





Consultation Pipeline Stages

Consultation Booked

 Client has booked a consultation via Calendly





Consultation Pending

 Consultation is scheduled but hasn't happened yet.





Consultation Completed

 Consultation has taken place. Tara fills out the Appointment Feedback Form to determine next steps.





Post-Consultation Paths Based on Client Interest

Client Interested in Coaching





Move to the Coaching Program Pipeline





Send coaching agreement and initiate onboarding





Client Interested in Treatments





Move to the Treatments Pipeline





Start the process for booking treatments or sending product recommendations





Client Interested in Both Coaching and Treatments





Add to both pipelines for parallel tracking





No Interest or Declined





No action unless they re-engage later





Follow-Up Required

 Waiting on a decision or clarification





Consultation Rescheduled

 Meeting moved to a later date





No Show

 Client missed the consultation — needs rescheduling or follow-up





 Where We’re Currently Stuck

We’ve hit a block in the Treatment Workflow — mostly because there are so many possible treatment combinations. The logic is getting complicated, and we need your expertise to:

Simplify or rebuild the workflow logic





Ensure multiple treatments trigger correctly





Send dynamic follow-up
s based on treatment selections





Make sure tags and custom fields update properly





????? What You’ll Be Taking Over

Continue building and improving this CRM system





Finalize all automation logic across pipelines





Fix the treatment workflow and handle multi-branch logic





Connect product/treatment flows to relevant
 sequences and follow-ups





Make the system scalable and easy to manage





Suggest and implement any smarter, cleaner strategies for better long-term performance







This is just our current foundation. We’re fully open to your ideas, improvements, or even reworking parts of the system if it creates a cleaner, more efficient setup moving forward.

title: Credit Repair Dispute Specialist (Experienced Only)
description:
Position: Credit Repair Dispute Specialist (Remote)



We are seeking an experienced Credit Repair Dispute Specialist to handle the full dispute process for U.S. credit reports. This role is strictly focused on credit dispute work. It is not a customer service or sales position.



Applicants must have proven experience disputing items with U.S. credit bureaus and demonstrate a clear understanding of the dispute process from start to finish.



Important:  

To be considered for this role, applicants must include verifiable proof of their experience and results. This may include links, case examples, screenshots, portfolio samples, or any documentation that demonstrates successful dispute work.  

Applications submitted without proof of experience will not be reviewed.



Responsibilities

- Review and analyze client credit reports from the major bureaus: Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion  

- Identify inaccurate, unverifiable, or outdated negative accounts  

- Draft and submit dispute letters and challenges to credit bureaus and creditors  

- Track dispute timelines and maintain organized documentation of submissions and responses  

- Provide clear records of disputes sent and outcomes when requested  

- Follow compliance guidelines related to U.S. credit reporting and dispute procedures



Requirements

- Proven experience specifically in credit repair dispute processing

- Strong understanding of dispute strategies and credit report analysis

- Ability to independently review credit reports and determine dispute opportunities

- Attention to detail and accurate record keeping

- Familiarity with the dispute process across all three major U.S. credit bureaus



Application Instructions

When applying, please include verifiable proof of your experience. Examples may include:

- A portfolio or documentation of dispute work

- Screenshots or records showing dispute activity and outcomes

- Links to previous projects or work related to credit repair disputes

- Any other proof demonstrating your hands-on experience with credit disputes



Applications that do not include clear evidence of dispute experience will not be reviewed.



This position is strictly for individuals with direct experience performing credit disputes. We are not currently hiring for customer service, sales, or administrative roles.



Please confirm that you have carefully read and understood the details of this role. In your response, kindly include the words “HOT START” so I know you’ve reviewed everything thoroughly.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
WFH

Graveyard Shift



Position Overview



We are seeking a highly experienced and proactive Executive Assistant to support a fast-paced executive leader. This role requires a self-starter who can anticipate needs, manage priorities independently, and operate without constant direction.



The ideal candidate has extensive experience supporting senior executives in fast-growing organizations, demonstrates *excellent organizational skills*, and can manage *calendar scheduling, travel arrangements, and executive priorities with minimal supervision*.



This is not an entry-level EA position. The executive expects a professional who can take ownership, solve problems independently, and keep operations running smoothly.



Key Responsibilities



Executive Support



* Provide high-level administrative support to the executive.

* Manage confidential and sensitive information with discretion.

* Act as a *gatekeeper and point of contact* for internal and external stakeholders.

* Anticipate executive needs and proactively resolve issues before they arise.



Calendar Management



* Manage the executive’s complex and constantly changing calendar.

* Schedule, prioritize, and coordinate meetings *without requiring constant approval or direction*.

* Arrange meetings with internal teams and *external stakeholders independently.



 Travel Coordination



* Handle end-to-end travel arrangements, including:



  * Flight bookings

  * Hotel accommodations

  * Ground transportation

  * Detailed travel itineraries

* Ensure travel plans are *optimized for efficiency and executive productivity*.



Meeting Coordination



* Organize meetings with *clients, partners, and external stakeholders*.

* Prepare meeting agendas, documentation, and follow-ups.

* Track action items and ensure timely completion.



Operational Support



* Assist with project coordination and executive initiatives.

* Maintain clear communication across departments.

* Manage competing priorities in a high-pressure environment.



Candidate Requirements

Experience

* 5+ years experience as an Executive Assistant* supporting senior leadership.

* Experience working in fast-paced organizations (preferably 150+ employees).

* Proven ability to support executives who operate at a *high level of speed and demand.



Skills & Competencies



* Exceptional calendar management skills.

* Strong travel booking and logistics coordination experience.

* Excellent organizational and time management abilities.

* Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

* Ability to *ork independently without being micromanaged.

* Highly proactive and solution-oriented.



# Work Style Expectations



* The executive does not spoon-feed instructions.

* The EA must anticipate needs and act without constant guidance.

* Must be comfortable working in a high-performance, fast-moving environment.



-- Ideal Candidate Profile



The ideal Executive Assistant:

* Is well-established in the EA profession

* Is not a beginner or learning the role for the first time

* Thrives in high-expectation environments

* Takes initiative and gets things done without being asked repeatedly



Send your resume to

title: Legal Assistant / Paralegal
description:
The Law Office of Natlie Figgers is seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Legal Assistant / Paralegal to support daily legal operations. The ideal candidate will assist attorneys with case preparation, legal documentation, client communication, and administrative support. This role requires strong organizational skills, professionalism, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced legal environment.



Key Responsibilities: 



- Assist attorneys with case preparation, legal research, and document drafting.

- May prepare, proofread, and file legal documents such as pleadings, motions, contracts, and correspondence.

- Maintain and organize client files, case records, and legal documentation.

- Handle calls.

- Communicate with clients regarding case updates, document requests, and scheduling.

- Manage attorney calendars, schedule meetings, hearings, and appointments.

- Coordinate with courts, opposing counsel, and other legal professionals.

- Assist in preparing materials for court hearings, depositions, and trials.

- Ensure all legal documents comply with court requirements and deadlines.

- Perform general administrative tasks, including
 management, filing, and data entry.



Qualifications: 

- Associate or Bachelor’s degree in Paralegal Studies, Legal Studies, or related field preferred.

- Previous experience as a Legal Assistant or Paralegal with US Law Firms is preferred.

- Strong knowledge of legal terminology and court procedures.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

- Proficiency in Microsoft Office and legal case management software

- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.

- Ability to maintain confidentiality and professionalism at all times.



Preferred Skills:

- Tech Savvy is a must

- Experience with legal document management systems.

- Familiarity with litigation procedures and court filings.

- Experience supporting attorneys in family law, personal injury, civil, corporate, or litigation matters.

title: Video Editor
description:
Video Editor | Full Time or Contract | Remote | Hacker Valley Media



We're a cybersecurity creative media agency behind one of the top podcasts in the space, Hacker Valley Studio (120K+ subscribers). We produce podcasts, video series, livestreams, and on-site events for leading cybersecurity brands.



We're looking for a video editor who thinks creatively, not just technically. You won't be following templates. You'll make real decisions about pacing, color, and storytelling.



You need to be:

• Expert in Descript and Adobe Premiere Pro

• Solid in After Effects and color grading

• Experienced in short-form content for TikTok, Reels, Shorts, and LinkedIn

• A self-starter who communicates clearly and delivers on time



Contract details:

• Rate: Negotiable. Tell us what you're worth.

• Hours: Flexible

• Starts with a 30-day mutual trial



To apply, send us:

1. Your portfolio or reel (podcast edits and short-form clips are a bonus)

2. A brief intro: who you are and why HVM

3. Your availability and rate

4. One question you'd want answered before we meet

title: Content Producer & Project Manager
description:
Content Producer & Project Manager | Full Time or Contract | Remote | Hacker Valley Media



We're a cybersecurity creative media agency producing podcasts, video series, and livestreams for leading brands in the industry. Our flagship show, Hacker Valley Studio, has 120K+ subscribers. We tell stories that make cybersecurity leaders look like heroes, and we need a producer who can own the content pipeline from start to finish.



This role is for someone who thinks like a producer first. You understand what makes content compelling, you can make creative decisions on the fly, and you know how to keep a production on track without sacrificing quality.



What you'll do:

• Own podcast and video production end-to-end, from raw recording to final deliverable

• Select the best 
nts from episodes and shape them into social-ready clips

• Make editorial calls on pacing, structure, and storytelling across formats

• Coordinate with editors, vendors, and specialists to hit deadlines

• Keep clients informed with timely updates and consistent follow-through

• Maintain project tracking, document workflows, and flag blockers early



What you need:

• 2+ years producing podcasts, video series, or similar content

• A strong editorial eye. You know what works and what to cut.

• Experience managing timelines across multiple projects simultaneously

• Confident client-facing communication and follow-through

• A self-directed work style. You take ownership and figure things out.



Nice to have:

• Descript, Adobe Premiere, or After Effects experience

• Familiarity with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini)

• Project management platforms (Wrike, Asana, Monday)

• CapCut or similar for quick social edits



What we offer:

• Remote, flexible work with a fast-growing media company

• Direct access to founders and leadership

• Real autonomy to shape creative direction and improve how we operate



To apply: Send a 3-minute video sharing your background, relevant experience, and what excites you about this role. Put "I've read this" in the subject line.

title: Advanced Estimating Assistant
description:
We are looking for ongoing, full-time support on the front end of our commercial construction projects. Day to day, you will jump directly into the architectural drawings, create accurate sketches in Xactimate X1, perform precise takeoffs and turn them into polished bid packages ready for submission. The goal is simple: We should be able to review and sign without needing major revisions.

Accuracy and speed are equally important. We need someone who can manage multiple projects at once while carefully verifying every line item. You must be comfortable working from PDF plans, building clean and organized sketches in Xactimate X1, and translating quantities into structured estimates using Google Sheets and EXCEL. All files are shared and managed through Google Drive, so familiarity with cloud-based collaboration is essential.

Typical deliverables for each project:

Setting up file in G Drive

Importing images into Xactimate

Completing Xactimate X1 sketch file

Draft bid document prepared for final review and signature

Equipment Requirements:

Laptop/Desktop

Minimum of 2 monitors

We handle commercial new builds and remodels, so experience with framing, paint calculations,MEP allowances, and finish schedules is a plus.  The ability to read blueprints is a must.

Communication is handled through Slack and
. The right candidate must speak and write fluent English to ensure clear communication with clients, suppliers, and internal tea
mbers.  

If you can commit to steady availability, fast turnaround times, high attention to detail, and a willingness to work a schedule based off of Central Standard Time in the U.S., let’s talk about the first set of plans waiting on our desk.

title: Media & Social Media Team - Remote (Philippines Only) - Healthcare Company
description:
Title



Media & Social Media Team – Remote (Philippines Only) – Healthcare Company







About the Company



From The Heart Home Care is a growing healthcare company based in the United States.



We are building a Brand Media Department and Social Media Operations Team that will produce high-quality content and grow our online presence across multiple platforms.



Our goal is to become one of the most recognized home care brands online.



We are looking for talented professionals in the Philippines who want to grow with a long-term company and help build something meaningful.







Available Positions



We are currently hiring for multiple positions.



1. Social Media Director / Media Manager



Responsible for managing the media team and social media strategy.



Responsibilities



• Manage content calendars

• Coordinate tea
mbers

• Review and approve posts

• Track analytics and growth

• Ensure consistent brand messaging







2. Content Strategist / Script Writer



Responsible for developing engaging content ideas and writing scripts.



Responsibilities



• Write scripts for short videos

• Develop weekly content themes

• Write captions and hooks

• Research trending topics







3. Short-Form Video Editor



Responsible for editing videos for social media platforms.



Responsibilities



• Edit TikTok videos

• Edit Instagram Reels

• Edit YouTube Shorts

• Add captions and motion graphics







4. Graphic Designer



Responsible for creating branded social media graphics.



Responsibilities



• Design carousel posts

• Create infographics

• Develop branded templates

• Design visual content







5. Community Manager



Responsible for engaging with our audience.



Responsibilities



• Respond to comments and messages

• Interact with followers

• Identify potential leads

• Maintain positive brand engagement







Platforms We Use



• Instagram

• TikTok

•

• YouTube Shorts

• LinkedIn







Starting Compensation



All roles start with a weekly starting pay during the evaluation period.



Role

Starting Weekly Pay

Media Director

$150

Content Strategist

$120

Video Editor

$100

Graphic Designer

$100

Community Manager

$90



All new hires begin with a 120-day evaluation period.



Compensation adjustments may be considered after this period based on performance, reliability, and contribution to the team.







Requirements



We are looking for candidates who are:



• Reliable and professional

• Excellent in written and spoken English

• Able to work remotely with strong communication

• Detail-oriented

• Creative and proactive



Experience with social media content is preferred.







Tools We Use



Experience with these tools is helpful:



• CapCut

• Adobe Premiere

• Canva

• Meta Business Suite

• Social media scheduling tools







Application Instructions (Important)



To apply, please follow these instructions exactly.



If these steps are not followed, your application will not be reviewed.

 1. Write “FTHHC MEDIA TEAM” in the subject line of your application.

 2. Tell us which position you are applying for.

 3. Provide links to work samples or portfolio.

 4. Record a 1-minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you would be a good fit for this role.



Also answer this question:



What is one idea you have for a social media video about caregiving that could go viral?



Work Schedule



Remote

Full-time preferred



Candidates must be able to communicate during U.S. business hours when necessary & weekends if applicable.







Long-Term Opportunity



We are building a long-term team and are looking for individuals who want to grow with the company.



Strong performers will have opportunities for long-term advancement within the media department.











Reply in OnlinePH message center with your answers to the following:



Media Team Applicant Screening Test



Before we schedule interviews, we ask all applicants to complete a short screening exercise.



This helps us identify candidates who are creative, detail-oriented, and serious about the role.



Please complete the following questions. This should take approximately 5 minutes.







Question 1 – Attention to Detail



To confirm you read these instructions carefully, please write this sentence at the top of your response:



“Great media teams focus on execution.”



Applicants who miss this step will not be considered.







Question 2 – Creativity Test



Imagine you are creating a 30-second social media video about caregiving.



Write a short idea for a video that could perform well on social media.



Your answer should include:



• The hook (first 3 seconds)

• The main message of the video

• The final message to the audience



Example format:



Hook:

Message:

Ending:







Question 3 – Caption Writing



Write a short caption for this topic:



“Signs your parent may need home care.”



Limit your caption to 2–3 sentences.







Question 4 – Platform Knowledge



Which social media platform currently gives the best organic reach for short videos, and why?



(Short answer)







Question 5 – Work Ethic



Tell us in 2–3 sentences:



Why do you want to work with From The Heart Home Care?







Final Step



At the end of your response, include the word:



“Execution.”



This confirms you carefully read the entire test.

title: SEO Specialist (Part-Time)

We are seeking an SEO Specialist to help improve organic visibility and search engine rankings through content optimization and SEO strategy.

title: Outbound Acquisition Specialist
description:
Property Seller Solutions is hiring a driven and disciplined outbound sales professional to generate new real estate opportunities through proactive calling and database follow-up.



This is a revenue-generating role focused on outbound activity, lead qualification, and appointment setting. The right candidate will be comfortable making a high volume of calls, navigating rejection, and building rapport quickly over the phone.



Key Responsibilities

- Make outbound calls to new and aged lead lists

- Identify property owners’ motivation, timeline, and decision-making authority

- Pre-qualify opportunities based on company criteria

- Set qualified appointments for acquisitions team

- Maintain accurate CRM documentation

- Follow structured call cadences and follow-up systems

- Assist in reactivating dormant opportunities in the database



Qualifications

- Strong verbal communication skills (solid English skills are required)

- High level of confidence on the phone

- Comfortable asking direct financial and property-related questions

- Highly organized and disciplined with follow-up

- Able to meet daily activity metrics and performance expectations

- Prior phone sales or call center experience preferred

- Real estate experience is a plus but not required



Performance Expectations

- 150–250 outbound dials per day

- Consistent talk time and meaningful conversations

- Appointment and qualification targets tied to performance bonuses



Compensation Structure

- Base pay

- Bonuses tied to qualified appointments and deal performance

- Growth path into closing the deals you set 



This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a production-based environment and wants a clear path to higher earning potential.



As part of our hiring process, we require all candidates to complete a Predictive Index (PI) assessment before moving forward to interviews. This helps us better understand your strengths and how you might fit within our team.

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
MUST APPLY AT LINK HERE: 



About Us

ValveMan, part of the FSW Group, is a family-owned, solution-focused distributor of valves and instrumentation with over 60 years of experience in the industrial, sanitary, and high-purity markets. We pride ourselves on expert service, high responsiveness, and a collaborative, supportive team culture.



Position Summary

The Executive Assistant provides day-to-day administrative and operational support to the CEO. This role is responsible for managing communication, scheduling, meeting coordination, and executive support tasks to help the CEO stay focused on priorities. The EA also partners with the Team Manager to offboard tasks to other virtual tea
mbers and ensures urgent matters are escalated quickly.



Key Responsibilities



Executive Support & Communication



Hold a brief daily sync with the CEO to confirm priorities and key updates.

Monitor inboxes and messages; read, draft, file/label
s, and respond to
s on behalf of the CEO as needed.

Unsubscribe from unwanted e-mails and or newsletters.

Flag and escalate urgent matters immediately and proactively keep the CEO informed.

Handle ad hoc tasks as requested



Calendar & Meeting Coordination



Coordinate, schedule, and book internal and external meetings.

Send invites, confirm attendees, manage reschedules/cancellations, and ensure meeting details are correct (time zones, links, agendas, and materials).

Join meetings as requested and note action items for each tea
mber through ASANA, request/require due dates for any task

Followup with tea
mbers and vendors on tasks assuring tasks are completed in a timely manner and following up, if required

Set appointments as requested (business and related personal/administrative appointments if applicable).

Set monthly and/or quarterly recurring meetings per schedule



Task Coordination & Delegation



Coordinate with the Team Manager to identify tasks that can be offboarded to other virtual tea
mbers.

Provide clear task notes, context, and follow-ups to ensure delegated work is completed accurately and on time.



Documents, Decks, and Reporting



Create and update decks and presentations (internal and external).

Build spreadsheets, pull basic reports, and complete ad hoc data/administrative tasks as needed.

Build SOPs and offboard as needed.

Summarize information clearly for quick executive review.



Instruction Intake & Follow-Through



Review Loom videos, voice memos, and written notes to capture instructions and convert them into clear tasks.

Confirm next steps, track action items, and ensure completion without repeated follow-up from the CEO.



Required Skills & Qualifications



Strong written communication skills (professional, clear, and concise).

Proficient and confident in speaking English.

Excellent organization and attention to detail; able to manage multiple priorities.

High responsiveness and strong judgment—knows what is urgent and escalates appropriately.

Comfortable supporting a fast-moving executive and handling confidential information.

Proficient with AI tools and common business tools 
, calendar scheduling, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365, spreadsheets, presentation tools).

Advanced skills in building and managing spreadsheets



Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have)



Experience supporting a CEO or senior executive.

Experience working with virtual teams and coordinating task delegation.

Familiarity with task/project tracking tools (e.g., Asana, ClickUp, Trello, 
).



Working Style Expectations



Proactive: anticipates needs and communicates early.

Reliable: follows through and closes loops.

Accountability: willing to hold tea
mbers accountable in a professional and supportive way

Escalation-first: urgent matters are always brought to the CEO’s attention immediately.

title: ???? Junior Media Buyer – Remote | Fast-Growing E-Commerce Startup
description:
?? Only apply if you have hands-on experience with paid media platforms (Meta preferred) and understand performance marketing fundamentals such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, and attribution models.



Please apply here: 
 



About Skinnify

At Skinnify (www.skinnify.co), we’re reinventing fitness by integrating resistance into everyday clothing. In just three years, we’ve grown from 0 to 150,000+ customers, and we’re just getting started.

To get a better idea of our company and what it’s like to work on our team, watch this short video:



We are now looking for a Junior Media Buyer to join our performance marketing team and help manage and scale our paid media campaigns.

You will primarily assist with campaigns for our US store, where we invest over $500,000 per month in paid advertising. This is a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience inside a fast-paced, high-growth e-commerce environment.



???? Your Responsibilities

? Assist in launching and managing paid campaigns across Meta, TikTok, AppLovin, Taboola, and other platforms

? Conduct A/B tests on creatives, audiences, funnels, and bidding strategies

? Support daily performance monitoring and reporting, using Triple Whale as the main source of truth

? Work closely with the creative and design teams to suggest improvements for ad creatives based on performance data

? Assist with basic conversion tracking and pixel setup when needed

? Monitor campaign metrics and identify opportunities to improve CPA, ROAS, and overall campaign efficiency

? Stay up to date with paid media trends, algorithm updates, and best practices



???? What We’re Looking For

?? 1+ year of experience in paid media buying or performance marketing

?? Hands-on experience with at least one major ad platform (Meta preferred)

?? Strong analytical skills and comfort working with campaign data

?? Basic understanding of key performance metrics such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, LTV

?? Familiarity with different attribution models

?? Strong attention to detail and ability to execute tasks quickly

?? Proactive mindset with a strong willingness to learn

?? Fluent in English



???? Why Join Skinnify?

???? Remote Work – Work from anywhere in the world

???? High Growth Environment – Join a brand that grew from 0 to 150,000+ customers in 3 years

???? Massive Ad Budgets – Work with campaigns investing $500K+ per month

???? Hands-On Learning – Gain real experience in scaling performance marketing campaigns

???? Work With Experts – Collaborate with experienced media buyers, creatives, and strategists

? Great Team Culture – Check out our employee reviews on Glassdoor:





???????? The Skinnify Story

Growing up, our founder Christian wasn’t the easiest kid.

His mom gave everything to raise him and his siblings - often neglecting her own health in the process. As an adult, Christian wanted to help her become fitter, but she simply didn't have time for the gym.

The solution came during his own workout. While using resistance bands, he thought:

"What if these were built into leggings?"

That idea became our first product: Resistance Band Leggings that turn everyday movement into exercise.

Since then, we've expanded into sleeves, shoes, and more, with the mission of helping busy women become fitter without needing the gym.



???? Skinnify’s Core Values

???? Innovation – We design groundbreaking clothing with built-in resistance.

???? Effectiveness – Our products help people burn more calories and get fitter with every step.

???? Accessibility – Fitness should be possible for everyone, even the busiest people.

???? Fun – Exercise should feel effortless and enjoyable.

? Quality – We build durable, high-quality products that last.



???? Ready to Grow With Us?

If you're passionate about performance marketing and e-commerce, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Applications Engineer
description:
Please only apply through this link: 





Company: FSW Group (Welsford and ValveMan)

Hours: Full-time, aligned with 8:00am - 5:00pm Eastern Time

Reports to: Sales / Technical Leadership

Compensation: $4-$8 per hour (based on experience)





-About Us



The FSW Group that is inclusive of Welsford and Valveman, is a family-owned, solution-focused distributor of valves and instrumentation with over 60 years of experience in the industrial, sanitary, and high-purity markets. We pride ourselves on expert service, high responsiveness, and a collaborative, supportive team culture.





-Position Summary



Our sales teams win by being fast, accurate, and consultative. The Applications Engineer is the technical backbone - you’ll collect the right process data, size/select/specify the right product, and help our team deliver the best customer experience in the valve and instrumentation space.





-Key Responsibilities



Technical intake: Receive information from the sales engineer around application details. (fluid, pressure/temperature, Cv/flow, materials, end connections, shutoff class, actuation, codes/standards, cleanliness requirements, etc.)



Product selection & specification: Recommend and specify the right solution across:



control valves, regulators, actuated valves, ball/butterfly/diaphragm valves



sanitary/high-purity valves and components



steam specialties (traps, PRVs, condensate) and safety relief (as applicable)



instrumentation tied to the application (basic accessories, gauges/transmitters where relevant)



Sizing & documentation: Perform or support sizing (as applicable), build clean submittals, and document assumptions clearly.



Expert for a few selected manufacturers (e.g. Jordan Valves, Steriflow)



Customer coordination: Work with customers as needed for technical specifications



Vendor coordination: Work with manufacturers/factories for technical confirmations, lead times, and quote support.



Sales enablement: Provide short, confident technical recommendations that help sales move faster 
-ready summaries).



Quality & high accuracy: Maintain clean records in our systems and to avoid doing re-quotes (product notes, selections, revisions, and key application data).



To generate quotes within the Prophet21 system for the outside sales team



Continuous learning: Become fluent in our line card and standard selection approach; build repeatable “playbooks” for common applications.





- Success Metrics



Strong product knowledge and selection skills across the FSW Group offering - especially control valves and regulators, with the ability to size, specify, and select the correct solution for the application.



Fast cycle time from inquiry ? recommended solution, with quick action and responsiveness to customer requests.



High accuracy (fewer re-quotes/returns/technical corrections) driven by clear, complete application data captured up front.



Strong vendor responsiveness, including timely follow-up and documented technical confirmations.



Consistent, professional write-ups the sales team can send directly to customers.



Team-first mindset - collaborative, reliable, and consistently living the company’s core values in a way that makes the sales team’s job easier.





- Required qualifications



Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical/Chemical/Industrial) or equivalent technical experience with strong valve/process aptitude



3+ years in valves/instrumentation/process equipment or proven applications/technical sales support experience



Ability to read/interpret P&IDs, datasheets, specifications, IOMs



Strong written English - able to produce clean, customer-ready summaries



Highly organized: you follow a process, document assumptions, and close the loop





- Nice to have



Control valve/regulator sizing familiarity (or eagerness to learn quickly)



Experience supporting pharma, food & beverage, OEM skids, utilities, or power



CRM/ERP experience (Pipedrive, QuickBooks, BigCommerce, or similar)



Familiarity with sanitary/high-purity standards and materials





- Tools & work style



Remote-first collaboration 
/chat/video) with U.S.-based teams



Comfortable working with structured templates and SOPs



Security-minded: follows strong password/device hygiene and company data practices

title: HR & Operations Coordinator
description:
Job Summary:



The HR & Operations Coordinator plays an active, multi-disciplinary role in the Company’s central team, primarily focusing on engaging applicants through available online tools and in-person interactions, conducting screenings and interviews, as well as assisting in the onboarding and training activities. This position works closely with other members of the HR, Compliance, Operations, and Payroll teams to ensure that we hire, retain, the best carers.



This role requires an experienced candidate who is a creative and critical thinker, is extremely organised, detail-oriented, and results-focused. The right candidate must possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, thrive in a fast-paced environment, and work equally well individually and in a team setting.



Minimum Qualifications:



- Bachelor’s Degree and at least one year of experience in recruiting, human resources, or business operations.



- Experience in health care or other service industries is preferred.



- Proven experience handling data or administrative workflows.



Essential Functions:



Recruitment & HR:



- Screening and interviewing applicants (conducting phone and video interviews).



- Conducting phone and
/text outreach campaigns to attract talent.



- Assisting in the onboarding and training activities for new hires.



Compliance:



- Assisting in the review and management of personnel files for all carers to ensure "audit readiness".



- Conducting due diligence checks, including chasing References, Right to Work documentation, and DBS (criminal record) checks.



Operations & Payroll:



- Collating and verifying timesheets from carers, ensuring accuracy before payroll processing.



- Preparing figures and entering data into payroll and rostering systems for senior sign-off.



- Assisting with the creation of weekly/monthly rotas and operational reports.



General:



- Participating and contributing to HR and Tea
etings.



- Assisting with other office functions and administrative tasks as necessary.



- Supporting the Company’s overall goal and mission of delivering quality care.



Knowledge, Skills, Abilities:



- A passion for the care sector and supporting our service users.



- Have knowledge of the UK care industry (preferred).



- Advanced computer skills, including proficient use of Microsoft Office 365. Must be highly skilled in Excel (formulas/data formatting), Word, SharePoint, and PowerPoint.



- General knowledge of employment laws and practices.



- A positive attitude with a passion for working with people and performing meaningful work.



- The ability to manage multiple tasks at once (e.g., recruitment and payroll deadlines) and prioritise goals.



- Ability to demonstrate critical thinking and resolution to daily challenges—a "systems thinker" who spots process improvements.



- Excellent oral and written communication skills and the ability to listen effectively.



- Sound judgement and decision-making skills.



- The ability to work independently to meet challenging goals and deadlines.



- Experience working collaboratively within a team of peers.



- The ability to demonstrate discretion, integrity, and fair-mindedness consistent with company standards, practices, policies, and procedures.



Success Factors:



- Positive, professional, business image.



- Ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words.



- Ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand (Clear, professional English).



- Ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.



- Ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.



- Ability to identify and understand the speech of another person.



- Ability to speak clearly so others can understand you.



This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. The employee will be expected to perform other reasonable related duties as assigned.



Working Schedule: UK Business Hours (e.g., 09:00 – 17:00 GMT/BST) Working Environment: Remote (Must have a dedicated home office setup with high-speed internet).



As a part of your application, please send a short video of up to two minutes introducing yourself and outlining your interest in the role to:

title: Marketing Professional (AI-Enabled Digital Marketing & Outreach)

This role is integral to supporting the firm’s growth by expanding digital presence, increasing leads, and ensuring consistent, strategic branding across platforms.

AI-Enabled Marketing & Content Creation

Generate and implement data-driven marketing campaigns, including
 marketing sequences and automated outreach flows.



Continuously research and recommend emerging AI marketing tools to improve efficiency and impact.



Social Media Management & Online Outreach

Manage and grow the firm’s presence on LinkedIn,
, Instagram, and other relevant platforms.



Create, curate, and post engaging content to build brand awareness and community trust.



Monitor analytics to evaluate engagement, reach, and conversion metrics, adjusting strategies accordingly.



Engage with followers, respond to comments, and support online community building.



Sales & Lead Generation Support

Assist in developing and implementing digital outreach strategies to attract potential clients and partners.



Collaborate with sales team to align marketing efforts with service offerings and strategic goals.



Develop landing pages, forms, and automations within CRM or marketing software to capture and nurture leads.



Branding & Communications

Ensure all marketing content and outreach efforts align with H&HS Consulting Group’s brand voice, mission, and equity-centered approach.



Support development of marketing collateral, slide decks, and proposal templates as needed.



Contribute to storytelling strategies that highlight client impact and the firm’s consulting expertise.



Required Qualifications & Skills:



Bachelor's Degree or above in marketing (digital, content marketing). 



Strong business leadership experience and skills relating to marketing campaigns and/or brand development.



Minimum 2 years of experience in digital marketing, AI-enabled marketing, or online outreach roles.



Advanced proficiency with Blaze.AI and familiarity with other AI marketing software (e.g., Jasper, Copy.ai, ChatGPT-4 for marketing workflows).



Demonstrated success in managing social media channels for brands or organizations, with knowledge of platform-specific strategies.



Excellent written and visual communication skills.



Strong understanding of lead generation, marketing funnels, and nurturing sequences.



Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects in a remote environment.



Commitment to professional ethics, equity, and representing mission-driven brands.



Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in marketing for consulting firms, nonprofits, healthcare, or human service organizations.



Familiarity with HubSpot CRM systems for marketing integration.



Basic graphic design skills using Canva, Adobe Spark, or similar tools.

title: Credit Dispute Specialist/ Repair Specialist
description:
We are looking for a Credit Repair Specialist.



We need someone who has a lot of experience removing collections, inquiries, charge-offs, bankruptcies, and all kinds of negative accounts from clients' reports.



Must be familiar with removing Chex systems, evictions, freezing the secondary bureaus, and tracking and reporting details.



We have additional education and training for you but we need someone who already has experience.



When applying, write "I am a credit repair beast" at the top of your application.



We need someone who can pay good attention to details on our client's credit reports.



Must be fluent in English.

Must have a backup power supply.

Must be able to work 5 days per week consistently.



In your application, please add in:

1) How long have you been doing credit repair?

2) How many clients profiles can you dispute in one day?

3) What credit repair strategies/techniques/methods are you familiar with?

4) What's the fastest you have been able to remove items from client's reports?

5) How do you handle inquiry removal?

6) Do you know a 4-day collection removal method?

7)How do you handle bankruptcies?

8) What softwares have you used for disputing?



Add anything else that you are proud of and that would be good for this position.

title: Accounting Manager, US Property Management  (Remote)
description:
*** Only candidates with 2+ years of Property Management Accounting experience will be considered ***



About Us

Streamline Property Management is one the largest and fastest-growing  professional property management firms located in the Washington DC area. We manage over 1,000 residential properties, delivering a seamless experience for both owners and tenants. As a fast-growing, entrepreneurial company, we move quickly and value tea
mbers who take initiative. We don’t just manage properties; we manage our clients' most valuable assets, and we pride ourselves on financial precision and transparency.



About the Role:

We are seeking a meticulous Accounting Manager to own the financial integrity of our residential portfolio. This is a specialized Trust Accounting role. You will be the guardian of funds belonging to owners and tenants, ensuring 100% compliance with state regulations and company standards. 

The Accounting Manager will be responsible for the financial integrity and reporting of the residential portfolio that Streamline manages on behalf of its owners. This role focuses on ‘Trust Accounting’ - the management of funds belonging to others (owners and tenants) and ensuring compliance with state real estate regulations and company standards.

Key Responsibilities:

Property Management Accounting 

- Owner Distributions: Execute monthly owner payment cycles. Verify clearing status, run financial diagnostics, calculate management fees, and execute ACH payouts. via AppFolio.

- Bank Reconciliations: Perform monthly three-way reconciliations between the bank balance, the software book balance, and the individual property ledgers.

- Closing the Month: Ensure all AppFolio diagnostics are cleared and books are "clean" before the new cycle begins.

- Tenant and Owner Transactions: Oversee security deposit refunds, move-out accounting, and daily ledger adjustments (move-ins, chargebacks, etc.).

- Process Security Deposit refunds and move-out accounting for tenants.

- Oversee Bookkeeping team to ensure invoices are entered correctly.



Process Management & Reporting:

- Improve upon and develop new processes to improve accounting and bookkeeping at the company with an eye to automate processes and use best practices from Appfolio and other companies

- Serve as the technical point of contact for complex owner statement inquiries.

- Empower Property Managers by providing clear financial context for client-facing discussions.

- Year-End Reporting: Prepare and distribute 1099s to vendors and owners; provide year-end financial packages for tax purposes.



Requirements:

- 3+ years experience in Accounting with minimum 2+ years in Residential Property Management accounting. Candidates with no prior experience in property management will not be considered

- Expert in AppFolio or Buildium - You should know the shortcuts, the reports, and the common pitfalls

- Strong understanding of Trust accounting principles

- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

- Fluent English, great written and verbal communication

- Self-starter and organizational skills



Compensation & Schedule

- Full-time, 40 hours per week, remote contractor position

- Opportunity to grow with a stable, expanding US-based company.

- Must be available to work US Eastern Time hours



Perks & Benefits

- Paid local public holidays (12+ days per year, depending on the official calendar)

- 15 paid vacation days annually (with potential to increase based on tenure)

- Opportunities for growth in both pay and responsibilities

- Be part of a supportive and friendly small team

- Stability and consistency - we are a long-term employer

- Work remotely from the comfort of your home
salary: Salary of US $10 to $15 per hour

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
Position Overview: 



As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of the executive office. Your primary responsibility will be to provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO, managing their schedule, and coordinating various tasks to enhance productivity. Your ability to maintain confidentiality, exercise sound judgment, and proactively handle administrative duties will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the CEO's office. 



 

Pay Range: $8 per hour with a performance review scheduled after 90 days of employment. We believe in rewarding exceptional talents. 



 

About Power Plus Systems: 



Power Plus Systems LLC has been a dominant force in the residential electrical contracting industry for two decades, and we continue to lead the way. Our vision is to successfully wire 28,000 homes to completion by the year 2029, ensuring safe and efficient electrical solutions, thereby making a positive impact on the lives of our customers and communities. At Power Plus Systems, LLC, a position with us isn't just a job; it's an opportunity to grow and develop within a company that genuinely appreciates your contributions and dedication to our shared mission. 



 

Objectives: 

• Manage and organize the CEO's daily schedule, including meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.

• Act as a gatekeeper, screening and prioritizing communications to the CEO.

• Coordinate and facilitate communication between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders.

• Anticipate the CEO's needs and proactively handle tasks to ensure optimal time management.

• Coordinate special projects and initiatives as assigned by the CEO.



 

Competencies: 

• Exceptional organizational and multitasking skills, with keen attention to detail.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive information.

• Proficient in using Microsoft Office and Google Suites.

• Familiarity with scheduling software; experience with additional productivity tools is an advantage.

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with individuals at all levels.

• Proactive problem solver and resourceful thinker.



 

Equipment Requirements:  

• Must own a reliable computer or laptop capable of handling administrative tasks efficiently.

• Must own and actively use an iPhone and/or MacBook or Mac computer for efficient communication and task management.

• High-speed internet connection to support video calls, cloud-based collaboration, and remote work.



 

Physical Requirements: 

• Extended periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

 

Benefits: 

• Growth opportunities in a well-established company.

• Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

• Opportunity for advancement within the company.

• A supportive and collaborative work environment.

• Company-observed paid United States holidays (full time).

• Paid vacation.





How to Apply: 



If you are ready to join Power Plus Systems, LLC, a well-established residential electrical contractor that values its tea
mbers and offers excellent benefits, please submit your application through the link below. 




/internationalcareers



Power Plus Systems, LLC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to apply. 



Join us at Power Plus Systems, LLC, and be part of our continued success as we embark on the next 20 years of excellence in residential electrical contracting!

title: Inbound Customer Service VA
description:
Position Summary

We’re looking for a reliable and well-spoken Customer Support Representative to handle inbound calls from clients and potential customers. You’ll address inquiries, guide leads through our internal systems, and ensure every interaction is handled professionally and positively. This role suits someone who is tech-savvy, steady under pressure, and comfortable with flexible or shifting work schedules.



Main Responsibilities

 – Handle inbound calls with a confident, friendly, and professional approach

 – Follow provided scripts while accurately entering caller details into the CRM

 – Schedule jobs quickly based on customer needs, giving priority to urgent or emergency cases

 – Adjust tone and pacing to match the caller’s mood and situation

 – Use local knowledge and postcode familiarity to maintain a natural, location-aware conversation

 – Coordinate with internal teams when necessary and escalate issues when required

 – Participate in ongoing training sessions and performance reviews



Required Skills

 – Clear, fluent English with a neutral or soft accent

 – Strong listening and communication skills

 – Ability to remain calm and effective during high-pressure or emotional situations

 – Strong attention to detail and consistency in following procedures

 – Comfortable with CRM systems, call handling tools, and team communication platforms

 – Dependable work ethic, consistent attendance, and openness to coaching



Preferred Qualifications

 – Previous experience in inbound customer service, dispatch, or call center environments

 – Familiarity with lead tracking and CRM software

 – Knowledge of UK or international postcode formats and area names

 – Quick to learn new tools and adapt to updated processes

 – Experience handling urgent or sensitive service inquiries



Technical Setup Needed

 – Reliable primary internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps recommended)

 – Backup internet option (e.g., pocket Wi-Fi or secondary provider)

 – Backup power source (UPS or generator) to avoid service interruptions

 – Personal laptop or desktop computer

 – High-quality noise-cancelling headset

 – Quiet, distraction-free workspace



Why You’ll Love This Role

Be part of a remote team where professionalism and clear communication are valued. You’ll receive structured scripts, ongoing training, and work in a no cold-calling environment, allowing you to focus on providing exceptional service. This role offers the chance to develop your skills in customer interaction and service coordination while working in a supportive, improvement-focused setting.

title: Operations & Administrative Coordinator
description:
Operations & Administrative Coordinator 

Independent Contractor (Remote) 



Engagement Type: Independent Contractor

Time Commitment: Full-time workload (approximately 40 hours per week)

Work Model: Fully Remote

Start Date: As soon as possible

Reports To: Founder & CEO



This role requires a full-time level of engagement, with an expected workload of approximately 40 hours per week. The contractor should maintain consistent availability during working hours to support daily operations and communication.

Work progress and task completion will be monitored through operational trackers, daily work plans, and end-of-day summaries.

About Skyang Bio

Skyang Bio is a U.S.-based biotechnology services company providing gene modulation and functional genomics solutions, including CRISPR libraries, ORF/cDNA clones, lentiviral packaging, and custom screening platforms. We support academic labs, core facilities, and biotech/pharma research teams across the U.S., Europe, and Asia.

Role Overview

This role supports the day-to-day administrative and operational activities of the company, ensuring internal workflows, communication, and tracking systems run efficiently.

The position focuses on maintaining operational organization, supporting executive coordination, and ensuring accurate tracking of business activities.

This is a remote, operations-focused role requiring strong organization, responsiveness, and accountability.



The role also provides support for internal sales coordination and lead generation activities to help maintain a healthy business pipeline.

Key Responsibilities

Operations & Administrative Support (Primary Focus)

• Support daily operational coordination and administrative activities

• Assist with scheduling and coordination of meetings and calendar management

• Prepare and send customer invoices based on completed services or project milestones

• Maintain accurate invoice records and track payment status from clients

• Follow up on outstanding or overdue invoices when necessary

• Maintain accurate internal tracking systems for:

o customer orders

o project status

o operational tasks

• Maintain and organize internal documentation and shared folders

• Assist with developing and maintaining internal standard operating procedures (SOPs)

• Provide regular operational updates and summaries to leadership

• Identify opportunities to improve internal workflows and operational efficiency

• Ensure internal tracking systems remain accurate and up to date

Invoice preparation, payment tracking, and overdue invoice follow-up are expected to be maintained accurately in the company’s operational and accounting records.

Internal Sales & Lead Generation Support (Secondary Focus)

• Support execution of Skyang Bio’s lead generation and outreach initiatives

• Follow and help improve Skyang Bio’s lead generation and
 engagement SOPs

• Research and compile qualified lead lists aligned with Skyang Bio’s services and target customers

• Execute outreach campaigns using approved
 templates and messaging

• Track outreach activities, responses, and follow-ups using internal tracking systems

• Maintain accurate records of leads, engagement status, and follow-up actions

• Achieve defined weekly
 outreach and engagement targets

• Monitor outreach performance and suggest improvements to
 engagement strategies

• Identify and flag warm or interested leads for internal follow-up

• Support communication and coordination with Skyang Bio distributors and external partners

• Maintain organized records of distributor contacts and communications

Outreach activity, engagement metrics, and follow-up actions are expected to be tracked and reported regularly.

Remote Work Expectations

Because this is a fully remote role, strong communication and responsiveness are essential.

The contractor is expected to:

• Monitor work
 throughout working hours

• Respond to internal and external
s within approximately one hour during working hours whenever possible

• Maintain proactive communication regarding progress and issues

• Notify leadership in advance if stepping away from work for more than 60 minutes during working hours

• Maintain organized and up-to-date operational records

• Actively manage tasks and responsibilities without requiring constant supervision

Daily Work Planning & Reporting

To maintain operational visibility in a remote environment, the role includes structured daily reporting.

Morning Work Plan

At the beginning of each workday, submit a short plan outlining:

• key priorities

• planned tasks

• any questions or items requiring guidance



End-of-Day Summary

Before the end of each workday, submit a short report including:

• tasks completed

• progress updates

• issues encountered

• questions or support needed

• priorities for the next day

Qualifications

• Experience in administrative support, operations coordination, or executive assistance

• Strong organizational and time management skills

• Excellent written communication skills

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

• Comfortable working with spreadsheets, trackers, and CRM tools

• Ability to work independently and manage responsibilities in a remote environment

• Strong attention to detail and accountability

Preferred:

• Experience supporting startup or small business operations

• Experience with CRM or lead generation systems

• Familiarity with
 outreach or sales support processes



Independent Contractor Relationship

This position is structured as an independent contractor engagement, not an employee position.

The contractor is responsible for managing their own schedule to meet the responsibilities and deliverables of the role.

Specific terms regarding work expectations, invoicing procedures, and payment schedules will be defined in a separate contractor agreement.



Compensation

Compensation for this role is $5 per hour.

This engagement begins with an initial evaluation period of approximately three weeks. During this period, the contractor will perform the responsibilities described in this role and demonstrate operational reliability, communication responsiveness, and the ability to manage tasks in a remote environment.

Continuation of the engagement after the evaluation period will depend on performance, including:

• responsiveness during working hours

• completion of assigned tasks

• accuracy of operational tracking

• adherence to workflow and reporting expectations

• demonstrating a proactive attitude in managing responsibilities and identifying improvements

• willingness to integrate into the company’s working culture and collaborate effectively with leadership

• openness to learning new skills and adapting to evolving operational needs



Application Instructions

To apply for this role, please submit:

1. Your resume or CV

2. A short paragraph describing your experience with administrative, operational, or remote coordination work

3. A brief explanation (2–3 sentences) describing how you organize and prioritize tasks when working remotely

To confirm that you have carefully read this job description, please include the phrase “Skyang Operations” in the subject line of your application
.

Applications that do not follow these instructions may not be reviewed.
